signature: squidpy.pl.ligrec(adata, cluster_key=None, source_groups=None, target_groups=None, means_range=(-inf, inf), pvalue_threshold=1.0, remove_empty_interactions=True, remove_nonsig_interactions=False, dendrogram=None, alpha=0.001, swap_axes=False, title=None, figsize=None, dpi=None, save=None, **kwargs)
description: Plot the result of a receptor-ligand permutation test.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object. It can also be a dict, as returned by squidpy.gr.ligrec().

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obs where clustering is stored. Only used when adata is of type AnnData.

params.name: source_groups
params.desc: Source interaction clusters. If None, select all clusters.

params.name: target_groups
params.desc: Target interaction clusters. If None, select all clusters.

params.name: means_range
params.desc: Only show interactions whose means are within this closed interval.

params.name: pvalue_threshold
params.desc: Only show interactions with p-value <= pvalue_threshold.

params.name: remove_empty_interactions
params.desc: Remove rows and columns that only contain interactions with NaN values.

params.name: remove_nonsig_interactions
params.desc: Remove rows and columns that only contain interactions that are larger than alpha.

params.name: dendrogram
params.desc: How to cluster based on the p-values. Valid options are: None - do not perform clustering., 'interacting_molecules' - cluster the interacting molecules., 'interacting_clusters' - cluster the interacting clusters., 'both' - cluster both rows and columns. Note that in this case, the dendrogram is not shown.

params.name: alpha
params.desc: Significance threshold. All elements with p-values <= alpha will be marked by tori instead of dots.

params.name: swap_axes
params.desc: Whether to show the cluster combinations as rows and the interacting pairs as columns.

params.name: title
params.desc: Title of the plot.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: dpi
params.desc: Dots per inch.

params.name: save
params.desc: Whether to save the plot.

params.name: scalebar_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for matplotlib_scalebar.ScaleBar().

params.name: edges_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for networkx.draw_networkx_edges().

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for matplotlib.pyplot.scatter() or matplotlib.pyplot.imshow().

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for scanpy.pl.DotPlot.style() or scanpy.pl.DotPlot.legend().

params.name: return
params.desc: Nothing, just plots the figure and optionally saves the plot.

misc: $molecule_1$ belongs to the source clusters displayed on the top (or on the right, if `swap_axes = True`, whereas $molecule_2$ belongs to the target clusters.

signature: squidpy.gr.mask_graph(sdata, table_key, polygon_mask, negative_mask=False, spatial_key='spatial', key_added='mask', copy=False)
description: Mask the graph based on a polygon mask.

params.name: sdata
params.desc: The spatial data object.

params.name: table_key
params.desc: The key of the table containing the spatial data.

params.name: polygon_mask
params.desc: The shapely.Polygon or shapely.MultiPolygon to be used as mask.

params.name: negative_mask
params.desc: Whether to keep the edges within the polygon mask or outside. Note that when `negative_mask = True`, only the edges fully contained in the polygon are removed. If edges are partially contained in the polygon, they are kept.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key which controls where the results are saved if `copy = False`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a tuple with the masked spatial connectivities and masked distances matrices. Otherwise, modifies the `adata` with the following keys: - anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{{key_added}}_{{spatial_key}}_connectivities'] - the spatial connectivities. - anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{{key_added}}_{{spatial_key}}_distances'] - the spatial distances. - anndata.AnnData.uns ['{{key_added}}_{{spatial_key}}'] - dict containing parameters.

misc: The polygon_mask must be in the same coordinate_systems of the spatial graph, but no check is performed to assess this.

signature: squidpy.tl.var_by_distance(adata, groups, cluster_key=None, library_key=None, library_id=None, design_matrix_key='design_matrix', covariates=None, metric='euclidean', spatial_key='spatial', copy=False)
description: Build a design matrix consisting of distance measurements to selected anchor point(s) for each observation.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: groups
params.desc: Anchor point(s) to calculate distances to. Can be a single or multiple observations as long as they are annotated in an .obs column with name given by cluster_key. Alternatively, a numpy array of coordinates can be passed.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Name of annotation column in .obs where the observation used as anchor points are located.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in anndata.AnnData.obs which stores mapping between library_id and obs.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied.

params.name: design_matrix_key

params.name: covariates
params.desc: Additional covariates from .obs to include in the design matrix.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Distance metric, defaults to 'euclidean'.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: return
params.desc: If copy = True, returns the design_matrix with the distances to an anchor point. Otherwise, stores design_matrix in .obsm.

description: Plot spatial omics data with segmentation masks on top.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: color
params.desc: Key for annotations in `anndata.AnnData.obs` or variables/genes.

params.name: groups
params.desc: For discrete annotation in `color`, select which values to plot (other values are set to NAs).

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in `anndata.AnnData.obs` which stores mapping between `library_id` and obs.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obsm` where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: img
params.type: bool | ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]] | Sequence[ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[Any]]] | None

params.desc: Key for image resolution, used to get `img` and `scale_factor` from `'images'` and `'scalefactors'` entries for this library.

params.desc: Colormap for the image, see `matplotlib.colors.Colormap`.

params.desc: Column in `anndata.AnnData.obs` with unique segmentation mask ids. Required to filter valid segmentation masks.

params.name: seg_contourpx

params.name: use_raw

params.name: edges_width
params.desc: Width of the edges. Only used when `connectivity_key != None` .

params.name: edges_color

params.name: library_first

params.name: frameon

params.name: hspace

params.name: outline_width

params.desc: Size of the figure in inches.

params.name: save

signature: squidpy.im.segment(img, layer=None, library_id=None, method='watershed', channel=0, chunks=None, lazy=False, layer_added=None, copy=False, **kwargs)
description: Segment an image.

params.name: img
params.desc: High-resolution image.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Image layer in `img` that should be processed. If None and only 1 layer is present, it will be selected.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied. If None, all Z-dimensions are segmented separately.

params.name: method
params.desc: Segmentation method to use. Valid options are: 'watershed'. Alternatively, any callable can be passed as long as it has the following signature: `numpy.ndarray (height, width, channels) -> numpy.ndarray (height, width[, channels])`.

params.name: channel
params.desc: Channel index to use for segmentation. If None, use all channels.

params.name: chunks
params.desc: Number of chunks for `dask`. For automatic chunking, use `chunks = 'auto'`.

params.name: lazy
params.desc: Whether to lazily compute the result or not. Only used when `chunks != None`.

params.name: layer_added
params.desc: Layer of new image layer to add into `img` object. If None, use `'segmented_{model}'`.

params.name: thresh
params.desc: Threshold for creation of masked image. The areas to segment should be contained in this mask. If None, it is determined by Otsu’s method. Only used if `method = 'watershed'`.

params.name: geq
params.desc: Treat `thresh` as upper or lower bound for defining areas to segment. If `geq = True`, mask is defined as `mask = arr >= thresh`, meaning high values in `arr` denote areas to segment. Only used if `method = 'watershed'`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the image container.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for the underlying model.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a new container with the segmented image in `'{layer_added}'`. Otherwise, modifies the `img` with the following key: `squidpy.im.ImageContainer['{layer_added}']` - the segmented image.

signature: squidpy.read.vizgen(path, *, counts_file, meta_file, transformation_file=None, library_id='library', **kwargs)
description: Read *Vizgen* formatted dataset. In addition to reading the regular *Vizgen* output, it loads the metadata file and optionally loads the transformation matrix.

params.name: path
params.desc: Path to the root directory containing *Vizgen* files.

params.name: counts_file
params.desc: File containing the counts. Typically ends with *\_cell_by_gene.csv*.

params.name: meta_file
params.desc: File containing the spatial coordinates and additional cell-level metadata.

params.name: transformation_file
params.desc: Transformation matrix file for converting micron coordinates into pixels in images.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Identifier for the *Vizgen* library. Useful when concatenating multiple `anndata.AnnData` objects.

params.name: return
params.desc:
Annotated data object with the following keys: 
- `anndata.AnnData.obsm['spatial']` - spatial spot coordinates in microns.
- `anndata.AnnData.obsm['blank_genes']` - blank genes from Vizgen platform.
- `anndata.AnnData.uns['spatial']['{library_id}']['scalefactors']['transformation_matrix']` - transformation matrix for converting micron coordinates to pixels. Only present if `transformation_file != None`.

misc:
SEE ALSO
- Vizgen data release program.
- `squidpy.pl.spatial_scatter()` on how to plot spatial data.

signature: squidpy.im.calculate_image_features(adata, img, layer=None, library_id=None, features='summary', features_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), key_added='img_features', copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True, **kwargs)

params.name: kwargs

description: Compute neighborhood enrichment by permutation test.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key

params.name: numba_parallel
params.desc: Whether to use `numba.prange` or not. If None, it is determined automatically. For small datasets or small number of interactions, it’s recommended to set this to False.

params.name: seed

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: n_jobs

signature: squidpy.gr.spatial_autocorr(adata, connectivity_key='spatial_connectivities', genes=None, mode='moran', transformation=True, n_perms=None, two_tailed=False, corr_method='fdr_bh', attr='X', layer=None, seed=None, use_raw=False, copy=False, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True)
description: Calculate Global Autocorrelation Statistic (Moran’s I or Geary’s C).

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: connectivity_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsp where spatial connectivities are stored. Default is: anndata.AnnData.obsp ['spatial_connectivities'].

params.name: genes
params.desc:
Depending on the attr:
> - if `attr = 'X'`, it corresponds to genes stored in anndata.AnnData.var_names.
>   If None, it’s computed anndata.AnnData.var ['highly_variable'],
>   if present. Otherwise, it’s computed for all genes.
> - if `attr = 'obs'`, it corresponds to a list of columns in anndata.AnnData.obs.
>   If None, use all numerical columns.
> - if `attr = 'obsm'`, it corresponds to indices in anndata.AnnData.obsm ['{layer}'].
>   If None, all indices are used.

params.name: mode
params.desc:
Mode of score calculation:
> - ’moran’ - Moran’s I autocorrelation.
> - ’geary’ - Geary’s C autocorrelation.

params.name: transformation
params.desc: If True, weights in anndata.AnnData.obsp ['spatial_connectivities'] are row-normalized, advised for analytic p-value calculation.

params.name: n_perms
params.desc: Number of permutations for the permutation test. If None, only p-values under normality assumption are computed.

params.name: two_tailed
params.desc: If True, p-values are two-tailed, otherwise they are one-tailed.

params.name: corr_method
params.desc: Correction method for multiple testing. See statsmodels.stats.multitest.multipletests() for valid options.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to access anndata.AnnData.raw. Only used when `attr = 'X'`.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Depending on `attr`: Layer in anndata.AnnData.layers to use. If None, use anndata.AnnData.X.

params.name: attr
params.desc: Which attribute of AnnData to access. See `genes` parameter for more information.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use. See joblib.Parallel for available options.

params.name: show_progress_bar
params.desc: Whether to show the progress bar or not.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If `copy = True`, returns a pandas.DataFrame with the following keys:
> - ’I’ or ‘C’ - Moran’s I or Geary’s C statistic.
> - ’pval_norm’ - p-value under normality assumption.
> - ’var_norm’ - variance of ‘score’ under normality assumption.
> - ’{p_val}_{corr_method}’ - the corrected p-values if `corr_method != None` .

If `n_perms != None`, additionally returns the following columns:
> - ’pval_z_sim’ - p-value based on standard normal approximation from permutations.
> - ’pval_sim’ - p-value based on permutations.
> - ’var_sim’ - variance of ‘score’ from permutations.

Otherwise, modifies the `adata` with the following key:
> - anndata.AnnData.uns ['moranI'] - the above mentioned dataframe, if `mode = 'moran'`.
> - anndata.AnnData.uns ['gearyC'] - the above mentioned dataframe, if `mode = 'geary'`.

misc: See Rey and Anselin, 2010 for reference.

signature: squidpy.im.process(img, layer=None, library_id=None, method='smooth', chunks=None, lazy=False, layer_added=None, channel_dim=None, copy=False, apply_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), **kwargs)
description: Process an image by applying a transformation.

params.name: img
params.desc: High-resolution image.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Image layer in `img` that should be processed. If None and only 1 layer is present, it will be selected.

params.name: library_id
params.desc: Name of the Z-dimension(s) that this function should be applied to. For not specified Z-dimensions, the identity function is applied. If None, all Z-dimensions are processed at once, treating the image as a 3D volume.

params.name: method
params.desc:
Processing method to use. Valid options are:
> - ’smooth’ - `skimage.filters.gaussian()`.
> - ’gray’ - `skimage.color.rgb2gray()`.
Alternatively, any `callable()` can be passed as long as it has the following signature:
`numpy.ndarray (height, width, channels) -> numpy.ndarray (height, width[, channels])`.

params.name: chunks
params.desc: Number of chunks for `dask`. For automatic chunking, use `chunks = 'auto'`.

params.name: lazy
params.desc: Whether to lazily compute the result or not. Only used when `chunks != None`.

params.name: layer_added
params.desc: Layer of new image layer to add into `img` object. If None, use `'{layer}_{method}'`.

params.name: channel_dim
params.desc: Name of the channel dimension of the new image layer. Default is the same as the original, if the processing function does not change the number of channels, and `'{channel}_{processing}'` otherwise.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the image container.

params.name: apply_kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `squidpy.im.ImageContainer.apply()`.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `method`.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If `copy = True`, returns a new container with the processed image in `'{layer_added}'.

Otherwise, modifies the `img` with the following key:
> - `squidpy.im.ImageContainer ['{layer_added}']` - the processed image.

misc: Raises: **NotImplementedError** – If `method` has not been implemented.

signature: squidpy.gr.co_occurrence(adata, cluster_key, spatial_key='spatial', interval=50, copy=False, n_splits=None, n_jobs=None, backend='loky', show_progress_bar=True)
description: Compute co-occurrence probability of clusters.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: spatial_key

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel jobs.

params.name: backend
params.desc: Parallelization backend to use.

signature: squidpy.gr.spatial_neighbors(adata, spatial_key='spatial', elements_to_coordinate_systems=None, table_key=None, library_key=None, coord_type=None, n_neighs=6, radius=None, delaunay=False, n_rings=1, percentile=None, transform=None, set_diag=False, key_added='spatial', copy=False)
description: Create a graph from spatial coordinates.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored. If adata is a spatialdata.SpatialData, the coordinates of the centroids will be stored in the adata with this key.

params.name: elements_to_coordinate_systems
params.desc: A dictionary mapping element names of the SpatialData object to coordinate systems. The elements can be either Shapes or Labels. For compatibility, the spatialdata table must annotate all regions keys. Must not be None if adata is a spatialdata.SpatialData.

params.name: table_key
params.desc: Key in spatialdata.SpatialData.tables where the spatialdata table is stored. Must not be None if adata is a spatialdata.SpatialData.

params.name: mask_polygon
params.desc: The Polygon or MultiPolygon element.

params.name: library_key
params.desc: If multiple library_id, column in anndata.AnnData.obs which stores mapping between library_id and obs.

params.name: coord_type
params.desc:
Type of coordinate system. Valid options are:
- 'grid' - grid coordinates.
- 'generic' - generic coordinates.
- None - 'grid' if spatial_key is in anndata.AnnData.uns with n_neighs = 6 (Visium), otherwise use 'generic'.

params.name: n_neighs
params.desc:
Depending on the coord_type:
- 'grid' - number of neighboring tiles.
- 'generic' - number of neighborhoods for non-grid data. Only used when delaunay = False.

params.name: radius
params.desc:
Only available when coord_type = 'generic'. Depending on the type:
- float - compute the graph based on neighborhood radius.
- tuple - prune the final graph to only contain edges in interval [min(radius), max(radius)].

params.name: delaunay
params.desc: Whether to compute the graph from Delaunay triangulation. Only used when coord_type = 'generic'.

params.name: n_rings
params.desc: Number of rings of neighbors for grid data. Only used when coord_type = 'grid'.

params.name: percentile
params.desc: Percentile of the distances to use as threshold. Only used when coord_type = 'generic'.

params.name: transform
params.desc:
Type of adjacency matrix transform. Valid options are:
- 'spectral' - spectral transformation of the adjacency matrix.
- 'cosine' - cosine transformation of the adjacency matrix.
- None - no transformation of the adjacency matrix.

params.name: set_diag
params.desc: Whether to set the diagonal of the spatial connectivities to 1.0.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key which controls where the results are saved if copy = False.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If copy = True, returns a tuple with the spatial connectivities and distances matrices. Otherwise, modifies the adata with the following keys:
- anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{key_added}_connectivities'] - the spatial connectivities.
- anndata.AnnData.obsp ['{key_added}_distances'] - the spatial distances.
- anndata.AnnData.uns ['{key_added}'] - dict containing parameters.

description: Calculate various Ripley's statistics for point processes.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obs where clustering is stored.

params.name: mode
params.desc: Which Ripley’s statistic to compute.

params.name: spatial_key
params.desc: Key in anndata.AnnData.obsm where spatial coordinates are stored.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Which metric to use for computing distances. For available metrics, check out sklearn.neighbors.DistanceMetric.

params.name: n_neigh
params.desc: Number of neighbors to consider for the KNN graph.

params.name: n_simulations
params.desc: How many simulations to run for computing p-values.

params.name: n_observations
params.desc: How many observations to generate for the Spatial Poisson Point Process.

params.name: max_dist
params.desc: Maximum distances for the support. If None, max_dist=\(\sqrt{area \over 2}\).

params.name: n_steps
params.desc: Number of steps for the support.

params.name: seed
params.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return the result, otherwise save it to the adata object.

params.name: return
params.desc:
If copy = True, returns a dict with keys:
- ‘{mode}_stat’ - DataFrame containing the statistics of choice for the real observations.
- ‘sims_stat’ - DataFrame containing the statistics of choice for the simulations.
- ‘bins’ - ndarray containing the support.
- ‘pvalues’ - ndarray containing the p-values for the statistics of interest.
Otherwise, modifies the adata object with the following key:
- anndata.AnnData.uns ['{key_added}'] - the above mentioned dict.
Statistics and p-values are computed for each cluster anndata.AnnData.obs ['{cluster_key}'] separately.

misc: For reference, Ripley's statistics can be found on Wikipedia or in the paper by Baddeley et al., 2015.

signature: squidpy.gr.ligrec(adata, cluster_key, interactions=None, complex_policy='min', threshold=0.01, corr_method=None, corr_axis='clusters', use_raw=True, copy=False, key_added=None, gene_symbols=None, **kwargs)
description: Perform the permutation test as described in Efremova et al., 2020.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data object.

params.name: cluster_key
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.obs` where clustering is stored.

params.name: interactions
params.type: DataFrame | Mapping[str, Sequence[str]] | Sequence[str] | tuple[Sequence[str], Sequence[str]] | Sequence[tuple[str, str]] | None
params.desc: Interaction to test. The type can be one of these specified formats. If None, the interactions are extracted from `omnipath`.

params.name: complex_policy
params.desc: Policy on how to handle complexes. Valid options are: 'min' for selecting gene with minimum average expression; 'all' for selecting all possible combinations between 'source' and 'target' complexes.

params.name: threshold
params.desc: Do not perform permutation test if any of the interacting components is being expressed in less than `threshold` percent of cells within a given cluster.

params.name: corr_method
params.desc: Correction method for multiple testing.

params.name: corr_axis
params.desc: Axis over which to perform the FDR correction. Only used when `corr_method != None`.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to access `anndata.AnnData.raw`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, return the result, otherwise save it to the `adata` object.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.uns` where the result is stored if `copy = False`. If None, `'{cluster_key}_ligrec'` will be used.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Key in `anndata.AnnData.var` to use instead of `anndata.AnnData.var_names`.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `copy = True`, returns a dict with following keys: 'means', 'pvalues', and 'metadata'. Otherwise, modifies the `adata` object with the key: `anndata.AnnData.uns` ['{key_added}'].

misc: NaN p-values mark combinations for which the mean expression of one of the interacting components was 0 or it didn’t pass the `threshold` percentage of cells being expressed within a given cluster.

signature: scanpy.neighbors.neighbors(adata, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=None, *, use_rep=None, knn=True, method='umap', transformer=None, metric='euclidean', metric_kwds=mappingproxy({}), random_state=0, key_added=None, copy=False)
description: Compute the nearest neighbors distance matrix and a neighborhood graph of observations. The neighbor search efficiency of this heavily relies on UMAP, which also provides a method for estimating connectivities of data points - the connectivity of the manifold (method=='umap'). If method=='gauss', connectivities are computed according to Coifman et al., in the adaption of Haghverdi et al.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Input annotated data matrix.

params.name: n_neighbors
params.desc: The size of local neighborhood (in terms of number of neighboring data points) used for manifold approximation. Larger values result in more global views of the manifold, while smaller values result in more local data being preserved. In general values should be in the range 2 to 100. If knn is True, number of nearest neighbors to be searched. If knn is False, a Gaussian kernel width is set to the distance of the n_neighbors neighbor.

params.name: n_pcs
params.desc: Use this many PCs. If n_pcs==0 use .X if use_rep is None.

params.name: use_rep
params.desc: Use the indicated representation. 'X' or any key for .obsm is valid. If None, the representation is chosen automatically: For .n_vars < N_PCS (default: 50), .X is used, otherwise ‘X_pca’ is used. If ‘X_pca’ is not present, it’s computed with default parameters or n_pcs if present.

params.name: knn
params.desc: If True, use a hard threshold to restrict the number of neighbors to n_neighbors, that is, consider a knn graph. Otherwise, use a Gaussian Kernel to assign low weights to neighbors more distant than the n_neighbors nearest neighbor.

params.name: method
params.desc: Use ‘umap’ or ‘gauss’ for computing connectivities. ‘umap’ relies on UMAP and ‘gauss’ uses the Gauss kernel according to Coifman et al.

params.name: transformer
params.desc: Approximate kNN search implementation following the API of KNeighborsTransformer. Behavior depends on data size. For small data, exact kNN is calculated, otherwise PyNNDescentTransformer is used. Also accepts 'pynndescent' for PyNNDescentTransformer and 'rapids' for a transformer based on cuml.neighbors.NearestNeighbors. Deprecated since version 1.10.0: Use rapids_singlecell.pp.neighbors() instead.

params.name: metric
params.desc: A known metric’s name or a callable that returns a distance.

params.name: metric_kwds
params.desc: Options for the metric.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: A numpy random seed.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: If not specified, the neighbors data is stored in .uns['neighbors'], distances and connectivities are stored in .obsp['distances'] and .obsp['connectivities'] respectively. If specified, the neighbors data is added to .uns[key_added], distances are stored in .obsp[key_added+'_distances'] and connectivities in .obsp[key_added+'_connectivities'].

params.name: copy
params.desc: Return a copy instead of writing to adata.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns None if copy=False, else returns an AnnData object. Sets the following fields: adata.obsp['distances' | key_added+'_distances'], adata.obsp['connectivities' | key_added+'_connectivities'], adata.uns['neighbors' | key_added].

misc: Examples and notes on the method and associated classes.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.scrublet_simulate_doublets(adata, *, layer=None, sim_doublet_ratio=2.0, synthetic_doublet_umi_subsampling=1.0, random_seed=0)
description: Simulate doublets by adding the counts of random observed transcriptome pairs.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes. Genes should have been filtered for expression and variability, and the object should contain raw expression of the same dimensions.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Layer of adata where raw values are stored, or ‘X’ if values are in .X.

params.name: sim_doublet_ratio
params.desc: Number of doublets to simulate relative to the number of observed transcriptomes. If `None`, self.sim_doublet_ratio is used.

params.name: synthetic_doublet_umi_subsampling
params.desc: Rate for sampling UMIs when creating synthetic doublets. If 1.0, each doublet is created by simply adding the UMIs from two randomly sampled observed transcriptomes. For values less than 1, the UMI counts are added and then randomly sampled at the specified rate.

misc:
#### SEE ALSO
`scrublet()`
: Main way of running Scrublet, runs preprocessing, doublet simulation (this function) and calling.

`scrublet_score_distribution()`
: Plot histogram of doublet scores for observed transcriptomes and simulated doublets.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.normalize_per_cell(data, *, counts_per_cell_after=None, counts_per_cell=None, key_n_counts='n_counts', copy=False, layers=(), use_rep=None, min_counts=1)
description: Normalize total counts per cell.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: counts_per_cell_after
params.desc: If `None`, after normalization, each cell has a total count equal to the median of the `counts_per_cell` before normalization.

params.name: counts_per_cell
params.desc: Precomputed counts per cell.

params.name: key_n_counts
params.desc: Name of the field in `adata.obs` where the total counts per cell are stored.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If an `AnnData` is passed, determines whether a copy is returned.

params.name: min_counts
params.desc: Cells with counts less than `min_counts` are filtered out during normalization.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an updated `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.X` : Normalized count data matrix.

misc:
### Deprecated
Removed since version 1.3.7. Use `normalize_total()` instead.
The new function is equivalent to the present function, except that

- The new function doesn’t filter cells based on `min_counts`, use `filter_cells()` if filtering is needed.
- Some arguments were renamed
- `copy` is replaced by `inplace`

### References
- **Seurat**: Satija *et al.*, 2015
- **Cell Ranger**: Zheng *et al.*, 2017
- **SPRING**: Weinreb *et al.*, 2017

### Examples

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> adata = AnnData(np.array([[1, 0], [3, 0], [5, 6]], dtype=np.float32))
>>> print(adata.X.sum(axis=1))
[ 1.  3. 11.]
>>> sc.pp.normalize_per_cell(adata)
>>> print(adata.obs)
   n_counts
0       1.0
1       3.0
2      11.0
>>> print(adata.X.sum(axis=1))
[3. 3. 3.]
>>> sc.pp.normalize_per_cell(
...     adata,
...     counts_per_cell_after=1,
...     key_n_counts="n_counts2",
... )
>>> print(adata.obs)
   n_counts  n_counts2
0       1.0        3.0
1       3.0        3.0
2      11.0        3.0
>>> print(adata.X.sum(axis=1))
[1. 1. 1.]
```

signature: scanpy.tools.tsne(adata, n_pcs=None, *, use_rep=None, perplexity=30, metric='euclidean', early_exaggeration=12, learning_rate=1000, random_state=0, use_fast_tsne=False, n_jobs=None, key_added=None, copy=False)
description: t-distributed stochastic neighborhood embedding (tSNE) for visualizing single-cell data. By default, implements scikit-learn's version. Offers speedup and better convergence if Multicore-tSNE is installed.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: n_pcs
params.desc: Use this many PCs. If `n_pcs==0` use `.X` if `use_rep is None`.

params.name: use_rep
params.desc: Indicated representation to use, e.g., `'X'`, or any key for `.obsm`. Automatically chosen if `None`.

params.name: perplexity
params.desc: Related to the number of nearest neighbors in manifold learning algorithms. Typically values are between 5 and 50.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Distance metric to calculate neighbors.

params.name: early_exaggeration
params.desc: Determines cluster spacing in embedded space. Controls tightness and spacing between clusters.

params.name: learning_rate
params.desc: Critical parameter; should be between 100 and 1000.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Seed for random initial states for optimization. If `None`, initial state is not reproducible.

params.name: use_fast_tsne
params.desc: Use fast tSNE implementation if available.

params.name: n_jobs
params.desc: Number of parallel computation jobs. `None` uses `scanpy._settings.ScanpyConfig.n_jobs`.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Overrides default storage locations for embedding and parameters.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Returns a copy if True, else writes to `adata`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns an `AnnData` object if `copy=True`, else `None`. Sets tSNE coordinates and parameters to the `obsm` and `uns` attributes.

misc: Installing Multicore-tSNE by Ulyanov (2016) improves speed and convergence.

signature: scanpy.get.get.rank_genes_groups_df(adata, group, *, key='rank_genes_groups', pval_cutoff=None, log2fc_min=None, log2fc_max=None, gene_symbols=None)
description: Get `scanpy.tl.rank_genes_groups()` results in the form of a DataFrame.

params.name: adata

### Example

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
```

signature: scanpy.read_h5ad(filename, backed=None, *, as_sparse=(), as_sparse_fmt=<class 'scipy.sparse._csr.csr_matrix'>, chunk_size=6000)

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.filter_genes_dispersion(data, *, flavor='seurat', min_disp=None, max_disp=None, min_mean=None, max_mean=None, n_bins=20, n_top_genes=None, log=True, subset=True, copy=False)
description: Extract highly variable genes.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: flavor
params.desc: Choose the flavor for computing normalized dispersion. If choosing ‘seurat’, this expects non-logarithmized data – the logarithm of mean and dispersion is taken internally when `log` is at its default value `True`. For ‘cell_ranger’, this is usually called for logarithmized data – in this case you should set `log` to `False`. In their default workflows, Seurat passes the cutoffs whereas Cell Ranger passes `n_top_genes`.

params.name: max_disp
params.desc: If `n_top_genes` unequals `None`, these cutoffs for the means and the normalized dispersions are ignored.

params.name: n_bins
params.desc: Number of bins for binning the mean gene expression. Normalization is done with respect to each bin. If just a single gene falls into a bin, the normalized dispersion is artificially set to 1.

params.name: n_top_genes
params.desc: Number of highly-variable genes to keep.

params.name: log
params.desc: Use the logarithm of the mean to variance ratio.

params.name: subset
params.desc: Keep highly-variable genes only (if True) else write a bool array for highly-variable genes while keeping all genes.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If an AnnData is passed, determines whether a copy is returned.

params.name: return
params.desc: If an AnnData `adata` is passed, returns or updates `adata` depending on `copy`. It filters the `adata` and adds the annotations. If a data matrix `X` is passed, the annotation is returned as `np.recarray` with the same information stored in fields: `gene_subset`, `means`, `dispersions`, `dispersion_norm`.

signature: scanpy.metrics.gearys_c(adata, *, vals=None, use_graph=None, layer=None, obsm=None, obsp=None, use_raw=False)
description: Calculate Geary’s C, which is a measure of autocorrelation for some measure on a graph. Lower values indicate greater correlation. Geary’s C is specifically used by VISION.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Input data matrix.

params.name: vals
params.type: ndarray or shape (n_features, n_cells) or shape (n_cells,)
params.desc: Values to calculate Geary’s C for. This can be selected from elements of the anndata object using keyword arguments `layer`, `obsm`, `obsp`, or `use_raw`.

params.name: use_graph
params.desc: Key to use for graph in anndata object. Defaults to neighbors connectivities if not specified.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Key for `adata.layers` to choose `vals`.

params.name: obsm
params.desc: Key for `adata.obsm` to choose `vals`.

params.name: obsp
params.desc: Key for `adata.obsp` to choose `vals`.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to use `adata.raw.X` for `vals`.

params.name: g
params.desc: The graph. Used if the function is called directly on the graph and values.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns a 1 dimensional ndarray if vals is two dimensional, or a scalar if `vals` is 1D.

misc:
Examples:

Calculate Geary’s C for each component of a dimensionality reduction:
```python
import scanpy as sc, numpy as np

pbmc = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_processed()
pc_c = sc.metrics.gearys_c(pbmc, obsm="X_pca")
```
Equivalent call directly on arrays:
```python
alt = sc.metrics.gearys_c(pbmc.obsp["connectivities"], pbmc.obsm["X_pca"].T)
np.testing.assert_array_equal(pc_c, alt)
```

signature: scanpy.plotting.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(adata, groups=None, *, n_genes=None, groupby=None, values_to_plot=None, var_names=None, gene_symbols=None, min_logfoldchange=None, key=None, show=None, save=None, return_fig=False, **kwds)
description: Plot ranking of genes using dotplot plot (see `dotplot()`).

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: groups
params.desc: The groups for which to show the gene ranking.

params.name: n_genes
params.desc: Number of genes to show. Can be a negative number to show downregulated genes (e.g. num_genes=-10). Ignored if `gene_names` is passed.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider. By default, chosen from the rank genes groups parameter, but other groupby options can be used. Expected to be categorical.

params.name: values_to_plot
params.desc: Plot values computed by `sc.rank_genes_groups`. Options: [‘scores’, ‘logfoldchanges’, ‘pvals’, ‘pvals_adj’, ‘log10_pvals’, ‘log10_pvals_adj’]. Use a divergent colormap for logfoldchanges.

params.name: var_names
params.desc: Genes to plot. Useful for checking specific var names' fold changes or p-values, instead of top/bottom genes. Could be a list or dictionary as in `dotplot()` or `matrixplot()`.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame storing gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Allows alternative names.

params.name: min_logfoldchange
params.desc: Filter genes in groups if logfoldchange is less than min_logfoldchange.

params.name: key
params.desc: Key used to store the ranking results in `adata.uns`.

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. Append string to default filename. Infer filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: return_fig
params.desc: Returns `DotPlot` object if `True`, else if `show` is false, return axes dict. Useful for fine-tuning the plot.

params.name: **kwds
params.desc: Passed to `dotplot()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns a `DotPlot` object if `return_fig` is `True`, else if `show` is false, return axes dict.

misc:
### Examples

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(adata, 'bulk_labels', n_genes=adata.raw.shape[1])
```

Plot top 2 genes per group.

```python
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(adata,n_genes=2)
```

Plot with scaled expressions for easier identification of differences.

```python
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(adata, n_genes=2, standard_scale='var')
```

Plot `logfoldchanges` instead of gene expression. In this case a diverging colormap
like `bwr` or `seismic` works better. To center the colormap in zero, the minimum
and maximum values to plot are set to -4 and 4 respectively.
Also, only genes with a log fold change of 3 or more are shown.

```python
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(
    adata,
    n_genes=4,
    values_to_plot="logfoldchanges", cmap='bwr',
    vmin=-4,
    vmax=4,
    min_logfoldchange=3,
    colorbar_title='log fold change'
)
```

Also, the last genes can be plotted. This can be useful to identify genes
that are lowly expressed in a group. For this `n_genes=-4` is used

```python
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(
    adata,
    n_genes=-4,
    values_to_plot="logfoldchanges",
    cmap='bwr',
    vmin=-4,
    vmax=4,
    min_logfoldchange=3,
    colorbar_title='log fold change',
)
```

A list specific genes can be given to check their log fold change. If a
dictionary, the dictionary keys will be added as labels in the plot.

```python
var_names = {'T-cell': ['CD3D', 'CD3E', 'IL32'],
              'B-cell': ['CD79A', 'CD79B', 'MS4A1'],
              'myeloid': ['CST3', 'LYZ'] }
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(
    adata,
    var_names=var_names,
    values_to_plot="logfoldchanges",
    cmap='bwr',
    vmin=-4,
    vmax=4,
    min_logfoldchange=3,
    colorbar_title='log fold change',
)
```

`tl.rank_genes_groups`

params.name: gprofiler_kwargs

Some useful options are `no_evidences=False` which reports gene intersections, `sources=['GO:BP']` which limits gene sets to only GO biological processes and `all_results=True` which returns all results including the non-significant ones.

params.name: return
params.desc: Dataframe of enrichment results.

misc:
### Examples

Using `sc.queries.enrich` on a list of genes:

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> sc.queries.enrich(["KLF4", "PAX5", "SOX2", "NANOG"], org="hsapiens")
>>> sc.queries.enrich(
...     {"set1": ["KLF4", "PAX5"], "set2": ["SOX2", "NANOG"]}, org="hsapiens"
... )
```

Using `sc.queries.enrich` on an AnnData object:

```pycon
>>> pbmcs = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
>>> sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(pbmcs, "bulk_labels")
>>> sc.queries.enrich(pbmcs, "CD34+")
```

signature: scanpy.plotting.dotplot(adata, var_names, groupby, *, use_raw=None, log=False, num_categories=7, categories_order=None, expression_cutoff=0.0, mean_only_expressed=False, standard_scale=None, title=None, colorbar_title='Mean expression\\nin group', size_title='Fraction of cells\\nin group (%)', figsize=None, dendrogram=False, gene_symbols=None, var_group_positions=None, var_group_labels=None, var_group_rotation=None, layer=None, swap_axes=False, dot_color_df=None, show=None, save=None, ax=None, return_fig=False, vmin=None, vmax=None, vcenter=None, norm=None, cmap='Reds', dot_max=None, dot_min=None, smallest_dot=0.0, **kwds)
description: Make a *dot plot* of the expression values of `var_names`. For each var_name and each `groupby` category a dot is plotted. Each dot represents two values: mean expression within each category (visualized by color) and fraction of cells expressing the `var_name` in the category (visualized by the size of the dot). If `groupby` is not given, the dotplot assumes that all data belongs to a single category.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: var_names
params.desc: `var_names` should be a valid subset of `adata.var_names`. If `var_names` is a mapping, then the key is used as label to group the values (see `var_group_labels`). The mapping values should be sequences of valid `adata.var_names`. In this case either coloring or ‘brackets’ are used for the grouping of var names depending on the plot. When `var_names` is a mapping, then the `var_group_labels` and `var_group_positions` are set.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present.

params.name: log
params.desc: Plot on logarithmic axis.

params.name: num_categories
params.desc: Only used if groupby observation is not categorical. This value determines the number of groups into which the groupby observation should be subdivided.

params.name: categories_order
params.desc: Order in which to show the categories. Note: add_dendrogram or add_totals can change the categories order.

params.name: expression_cutoff
params.desc: Expression cutoff that is used for binarizing the gene expression and determining the fraction of cells expressing given genes. A gene is expressed only if the expression value is greater than this threshold.

params.name: mean_only_expressed
params.desc: If True, gene expression is averaged only over the cells expressing the given genes.

params.name: standard_scale
params.desc: Whether or not to standardize the given dimension between 0 and 1, meaning for each variable or group, subtract the minimum and divide each by its maximum.

params.name: title
params.desc: Title for the figure.

params.name: colorbar_title
params.desc: Title for the color bar. New line character (n) can be used.

params.name: size_title
params.desc: Title for the size legend. New line character (n) can be used.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Figure size when `multi_panel=True`. Otherwise the `rcParam['figure.figsize]` value is used. Format is (width, height).

params.name: dendrogram
params.desc: If True or a valid dendrogram key, a dendrogram based on the hierarchical clustering between the `groupby` categories is added. The dendrogram information is computed using `scanpy.tl.dendrogram()`. If `tl.dendrogram` has not been called previously the function is called with default parameters.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: var_group_positions
params.desc: Use this parameter to highlight groups of `var_names`. This will draw a ‘bracket’ or a color block between the given start and end positions. If the parameter `var_group_labels` is set, the corresponding labels are added on top/left. E.g. `var_group_positions=[(4,10)]` will add a bracket between the fourth `var_name` and the tenth `var_name`. By giving more positions, more brackets/color blocks are drawn.

params.name: var_group_labels
params.desc: Labels for each of the `var_group_positions` that want to be highlighted.

params.name: var_group_rotation
params.desc: Label rotation degrees. By default, labels larger than 4 characters are rotated 90 degrees.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Name of the AnnData object layer that wants to be plotted. By default adata.raw.X is plotted. If `use_raw=False` is set, then `adata.X` is plotted. If `layer` is set to a valid layer name, then the layer is plotted. `layer` takes precedence over `use_raw`.

params.name: swap_axes
params.desc: By default, the x axis contains `var_names` (e.g. genes) and the y axis the `groupby` categories. By setting `swap_axes` then x are the `groupby` categories and y the `var_names`.

params.name: return_fig
params.desc: Returns `DotPlot` object. Useful for fine-tuning the plot. Takes precedence over `show=False`.

params.name: dot_color_df
params.desc: Optional DataFrame with dot colors.

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: vmin
params.desc: The value representing the lower limit of the color scale. Values smaller than vmin are plotted with the same color as vmin.

params.name: vmax
params.desc: The value representing the upper limit of the color scale. Values larger than vmax are plotted with the same color as vmax.

params.name: vcenter
params.desc: The value representing the center of the color scale. Useful for diverging colormaps.

params.name: norm
params.desc: Custom color normalization object from matplotlib.

params.name: cmap
params.desc: String denoting matplotlib color map.

params.name: dot_max
params.desc: If `None`, the maximum dot size is set to the maximum fraction value found (e.g. 0.6). If given, the value should be a number between 0 and 1. All fractions larger than dot_max are clipped to this value.

params.name: dot_min
params.desc: If `None`, the minimum dot size is set to 0. If given, the value should be a number between 0 and 1. All fractions smaller than dot_min are clipped to this value.

params.name: smallest_dot
params.desc: All expression levels with `dot_min` are plotted with this size.

params.name: kwds
params.desc: Passed to `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `return_fig` is `True`, returns a `DotPlot` object, else if `show` is false, return axes dict

misc:
### Examples

Create a dot plot using the given markers and the PBMC example dataset grouped by the category ‘bulk_labels’.

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
markers = ['C1QA', 'PSAP', 'CD79A', 'CD79B', 'CST3', 'LYZ']
```

signature: scanpy.tools.rank_genes_groups(adata, groupby, *, mask_var=None, use_raw=None, groups='all', reference='rest', n_genes=None, rankby_abs=False, pts=False, key_added=None, copy=False, method=None, corr_method='benjamini-hochberg', tie_correct=False, layer=None, **kwds)
description: Rank genes for characterizing groups. Expects logarithmized data.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observations grouping to consider.

params.name: mask_var
params.desc: Select subset of genes to use in statistical tests.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present. The default behavior is to use `raw` if present.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Key from `adata.layers` whose value will be used to perform tests on.

params.name: groups
params.desc: Subset of groups to which comparison shall be restricted, or `'all'` (default), for all groups.

params.name: reference
params.desc: If `'rest'`, compare each group to the union of the rest of the group. If a group identifier, compare with respect to this group.

params.name: n_genes
params.desc: The number of genes that appear in the returned tables. Defaults to all genes.

params.name: method
params.desc: The method to be used for ranking. Options are `'t-test'`, `'t-test_overestim_var'`, `'wilcoxon'`, `'logreg'`.

params.name: corr_method
params.desc: p-value correction method. Used only for `'t-test'`, `'t-test_overestim_var'`, and `'wilcoxon'`.

params.name: tie_correct
params.desc: Use tie correction for `'wilcoxon'` scores. Used only for `'wilcoxon'`.

params.name: rankby_abs
params.desc: Rank genes by the absolute value of the score, not by the score. The returned scores are never the absolute values.

params.name: pts
params.desc: Compute the fraction of cells expressing the genes.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: The key in `adata.uns` information is saved to.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Whether to copy `adata` or modify it inplace.

params.name: kwds
params.desc: Parameters that are passed to test methods.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object.

misc:
Sets the following fields:

- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['names']`: Structured array to be indexed by group id storing the gene names.
- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['scores']`: Structured array to be indexed by group id storing the z-score underlying the computation of a p-value for each gene for each group.
- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['logfoldchanges']`: Structured array to be indexed by group id storing the log2 fold change for each gene for each group.
- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['pvals']`: p-values.
- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['pvals_adj']`: Corrected p-values.
- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['pts']`: Fraction of cells expressing the genes for each group.
- `adata.uns['rank_genes_groups' | key_added]['pts_rest']`: Only if `reference` is set to `'rest'`. Fraction of cells from the union of the rest of each group expressing the genes.

### Notes
There are slight inconsistencies depending on whether sparse or dense data are passed.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.neighbors(adata, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=None, *, use_rep=None, knn=True, method='umap', transformer=None, metric='euclidean', metric_kwds=mappingproxy({}), random_state=0, key_added=None, copy=False)
description: Compute the nearest neighbors distance matrix and a neighborhood graph of observations. The neighbor search efficiency heavily relies on UMAP, which also provides a method for estimating connectivities of data points - the connectivity of the manifold (`method=='umap'`). If `method=='gauss'`, connectivities are computed according to Coifman *et al.* [2005], in the adaption of Haghverdi *et al.* [2016].

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: n_neighbors
params.desc: The size of local neighborhood (in terms of number of neighboring data points) used for manifold approximation. Larger values result in more global views of the manifold, while smaller values result in more local data being preserved. In general, values should be in the range 2 to 100. If `knn` is `True`, number of nearest neighbors to be searched. If `knn` is `False`, a Gaussian kernel width is set to the distance of the `n_neighbors` neighbor.

params.name: n_pcs
params.desc: Use this many PCs. If `n_pcs==0` use `.X` if `use_rep is None`.

params.name: use_rep
params.desc: Use the indicated representation. `'X'` or any key for `.obsm` is valid. If `None`, the representation is chosen automatically based on `.n_vars` and `N_PCS`.

params.name: knn
params.desc: If `True`, use a hard threshold to restrict the number of neighbors to `n_neighbors`. Otherwise, use a Gaussian Kernel to assign low weights to neighbors more distant than the `n_neighbors` nearest neighbor.

params.name: method
params.desc: Use ‘umap’ or ‘gauss’ for computing connectivities.

params.name: transformer
params.desc: Approximate kNN search implementation. Behavior depends on data size. Also accepts known options: `None`, `'pynndescent'`, `'rapids'`.

params.name: metric
params.desc: A known metric’s name or a callable that returns a distance.

params.name: metric_kwds
params.desc: Options for the metric.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: A numpy random seed.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: If not specified, neighbors data is stored in `.uns['neighbors']`. If specified, neighbors data is added to `.uns[key_added]`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Return a copy instead of writing to adata.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets fields: `adata.obsp['distances' | key_added+'_distances']`, `adata.obsp['connectivities' | key_added+'_connectivities']`, and `adata.uns['neighbors' | key_added]`.

misc:
### Examples

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
>>> # Basic usage
>>> sc.pp.neighbors(adata, 20, metric="cosine")
>>> # Provide your own transformer for more control and flexibility
>>> from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsTransformer
>>> transformer = KNeighborsTransformer(
...     n_neighbors=10, metric="manhattan", algorithm="kd_tree"
... )
>>> sc.pp.neighbors(adata, transformer=transformer)
>>> # now you can e.g. access the index: `transformer._tree`
```

signature: scanpy.plotting.rank_genes_groups_stacked_violin(adata, groups=None, *, n_genes=None, groupby=None, gene_symbols=None, var_names=None, min_logfoldchange=None, key=None, show=None, save=None, return_fig=False, **kwds)
description: Plot ranking of genes using stacked_violin plot.

params.name: adata

params.name: groups
params.desc: The groups for which to show the gene ranking.

params.name: n_genes
params.desc: Number of genes to show. This can be a negative number to show for example the down regulated genes. eg: num_genes=-10. Is ignored if `gene_names` is passed.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider. By default, the groupby is chosen from the rank genes groups parameter but other groupby options can be used. It is expected that groupby is a categorical. If groupby is not a categorical observation, it would be subdivided into `num_categories`.

params.name: gene_symbols

params.name: key

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: return_fig
params.desc: Returns `StackedViolin` object. Useful for fine-tuning the plot. Takes precedence over `show=False`.

params.name: **kwds
params.desc: Are passed to `stacked_violin()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `return_fig` is `True`, returns a `StackedViolin` object, else if `show` is false, return axes dict.

misc:
### Examples

```pycon
>>> adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()

```

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.scrublet(adata, adata_sim=None, *, batch_key=None, sim_doublet_ratio=2.0, expected_doublet_rate=0.05, stdev_doublet_rate=0.02, synthetic_doublet_umi_subsampling=1.0, knn_dist_metric='euclidean', normalize_variance=True, log_transform=False, mean_center=True, n_prin_comps=30, use_approx_neighbors=None, get_doublet_neighbor_parents=False, n_neighbors=None, threshold=None, verbose=True, copy=False, random_state=0)
description: Predict doublets using Scrublet. Predict cell doublets using a nearest-neighbor classifier of observed transcriptomes and simulated doublets. Works best if the input is a raw (unnormalized) counts matrix from a single sample or a collection of similar samples from the same experiment. This function is a wrapper around functions that pre-process using Scanpy and directly call functions of Scrublet(). You may also undertake your own preprocessing, simulate doublets with `scrublet_simulate_doublets()`, and run the core scrublet function `scrublet()` with `adata_sim` set.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes. Expected to be un-normalised where adata_sim is not supplied, in which case doublets will be simulated and pre-processing applied to both objects. If adata_sim is supplied, this should be the observed transcriptomes processed consistently (filtering, transform, normalization, hvg) with adata_sim.

params.name: adata_sim
params.desc: (Advanced use case) Optional AnnData object generated by `scrublet_simulate_doublets()`, with same number of vars as adata. This should have been built from adata_obs after filtering genes and cells and selecting highly-variable genes.

params.name: batch_key
params.desc: Optional `obs` column name discriminating between batches.

params.name: sim_doublet_ratio
params.desc: Number of doublets to simulate relative to the number of observed transcriptomes.

params.name: expected_doublet_rate
params.desc: Where adata_sim not supplied, the estimated doublet rate for the experiment.

params.name: stdev_doublet_rate
params.desc: Where adata_sim not supplied, uncertainty in the expected doublet rate.

params.name: synthetic_doublet_umi_subsampling
params.desc: Where adata_sim not supplied, rate for sampling UMIs when creating synthetic doublets. If 1.0, each doublet is created by simply adding the UMI counts from two randomly sampled observed transcriptomes. For values less than 1, the UMI counts are added and then randomly sampled at the specified rate.

params.name: knn_dist_metric
params.desc: Distance metric used when finding nearest neighbors. For list of valid values, see the documentation for annoy (if `use_approx_neighbors` is True) or sklearn.neighbors.NearestNeighbors (if `use_approx_neighbors` is False).

params.name: normalize_variance
params.desc: If True, normalize the data such that each gene has a variance of 1. sklearn.decomposition.TruncatedSVD will be used for dimensionality reduction, unless `mean_center` is True.

params.name: log_transform
params.desc: Whether to use `log1p()` to log-transform the data prior to PCA.

params.name: mean_center
params.desc: If True, center the data such that each gene has a mean of 0. sklearn.decomposition.PCA will be used for dimensionality reduction.

params.name: n_prin_comps
params.desc: Number of principal components used to embed the transcriptomes prior to k-nearest-neighbor graph construction.

params.name: use_approx_neighbors
params.desc: Use approximate nearest neighbor method (annoy) for the KNN classifier.

params.name: get_doublet_neighbor_parents
params.desc: If True, return (in .uns) the parent transcriptomes that generated the doublet neighbors of each observed transcriptome. This information can be used to infer the cell states that generated a given doublet state.

params.name: n_neighbors
params.desc: Number of neighbors used to construct the KNN graph of observed transcriptomes and simulated doublets. If `None`, this is automatically set to `np.round(0.5 * np.sqrt(n_obs))`.

params.name: threshold
params.desc: Doublet score threshold for calling a transcriptome a doublet. If `None`, this is set automatically by looking for the minimum between the two modes of the `doublet_scores_sim_` histogram. It is best practice to check the threshold visually using the `doublet_scores_sim_` histogram and/or based on co-localization of predicted doublets in a 2-D embedding.

params.name: verbose
params.desc: If True, log progress updates.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If True, return a copy of the input `adata` with Scrublet results added. Otherwise, Scrublet results are added in place.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Initial state for doublet simulation and nearest neighbors.

params.name: return
params.desc: if `copy=True` it returns or else adds fields to `adata`. Those fields: `.obs['doublet_score']`: Doublet scores for each observed transcriptome `.obs['predicted_doublet']`: Boolean indicating predicted doublet status `.uns['scrublet']['doublet_scores_sim']`: Doublet scores for each simulated doublet transcriptome `.uns['scrublet']['doublet_parents']`: Pairs of `.obs_names` used to generate each simulated doublet transcriptome `.uns['scrublet']['parameters']`: Dictionary of Scrublet parameters

misc:
#### SEE ALSO
`scrublet_simulate_doublets()`
: Run Scrublet’s doublet simulation separately for advanced usage.

`scrublet_score_distribution()`
: Plot histogram of doublet scores for observed transcriptomes and simulated doublets.

signature: scanpy.plotting.paga(adata, *, threshold=None, color=None, layout=None, layout_kwds=mappingproxy({}), init_pos=None, root=0, labels=None, single_component=False, solid_edges='connectivities', dashed_edges=None, transitions=None, fontsize=None, fontweight='bold', fontoutline=None, text_kwds=mappingproxy({}), node_size_scale=1.0, node_size_power=0.5, edge_width_scale=1.0, min_edge_width=None, max_edge_width=None, arrowsize=30, title=None, left_margin=0.01, random_state=0, pos=None, normalize_to_color=False, cmap=None, cax=None, colorbar=None, cb_kwds=mappingproxy({}), frameon=None, add_pos=True, export_to_gexf=False, use_raw=True, colors=None, groups=None, plot=True, show=None, save=None, ax=None)
description: Plot the PAGA graph through thresholding low-connectivity edges. Compute a coarse-grained layout of the data. Reuse this by passing `init_pos='paga'` to `umap()` or `draw_graph()` and obtain embeddings with more meaningful global topology. This uses ForceAtlas2 or igraph’s layout algorithms for most layouts.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: threshold
params.desc: Do not draw edges for weights below this threshold. Set to 0 if you want all edges. Discarding low-connectivity edges helps in getting a much clearer picture of the graph.

params.name: color
params.desc: Gene name or `obs` annotation defining the node colors. Also plots the degree of the abstracted graph when passing {'degree_dashed', 'degree_solid'}. Can be also used to visualize pie chart at each node in the following form: {<group name or index>: {<color>: <fraction>, ...}, ...}.

params.name: labels
params.desc: The node labels. If `None`, this defaults to the group labels stored in the categorical for which `paga()` has been computed.

params.name: pos
params.desc: Two-column array-like storing the x and y coordinates for drawing. Otherwise, path to a `.gdf` file that has been exported from Gephi or a similar graph visualization software.

params.name: layout
params.desc: Plotting layout that computes positions.

params.name: layout_kwds
params.desc: Keywords for the layout.

params.name: init_pos
params.desc: Two-column array storing the x and y coordinates for initializing the layout.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: For layouts with random initialization like 'fr', change this to use different initial states for the optimization. If `None`, the initial state is not reproducible.

params.name: root
params.desc: If choosing a tree layout, this is the index of the root node or a list of root node indices.

params.name: transitions
params.desc: Key for `.uns['paga']` that specifies the matrix that stores the arrows, for instance 'transitions_confidence'.

params.name: solid_edges
params.desc: Key for `.uns['paga']` that specifies the matrix that stores the edges to be drawn solid black.

params.name: dashed_edges
params.desc: Key for `.uns['paga']` that specifies the matrix that stores the edges to be drawn dashed grey. If `None`, no dashed edges are drawn.

params.name: single_component
params.desc: Restrict to largest connected component.

params.name: fontsize
params.desc: Font size for node labels.

params.name: fontoutline
params.desc: Width of the white outline around fonts.

params.name: text_kwds
params.desc: Keywords for `text()`.

params.name: node_size_scale
params.desc: Increase or decrease the size of the nodes.

params.name: node_size_power
params.desc: The power with which groups sizes influence the radius of the nodes.

params.name: edge_width_scale
params.desc: Edge with scale in units of `rcParams['lines.linewidth']`.

params.name: min_edge_width
params.desc: Min width of solid edges.

params.name: max_edge_width
params.desc: Max width of solid and dashed edges.

params.name: arrowsize
params.desc: For directed graphs, choose the size of the arrow head head’s length and width.

params.name: export_to_gexf
params.desc: Export to gexf format to be read by graph visualization programs such as Gephi.

params.name: normalize_to_color
params.desc: Whether to normalize categorical plots to `color` or the underlying grouping.

params.name: cmap
params.desc: The color map.

params.name: cax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object for a potential colorbar.

params.name: cb_kwds
params.desc: Keyword arguments for `Colorbar`, for instance, `ticks`.

params.name: add_pos
params.desc: Add the positions to `adata.uns['paga']`.

params.name: title
params.desc: Provide a title.

params.name: frameon
params.desc: Draw a frame around the PAGA graph.

params.name: plot
params.desc: If `False`, do not create the figure, simply compute the layout.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {'.pdf', '.png', '.svg'}.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `show==False`, one or more `Axes` objects. Adds `'pos'` to `adata.uns['paga']` if `add_pos` is `True`.

misc:
### Examples

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc3k_processed()
sc.tl.paga(adata, groups='louvain')
sc.pl.paga(adata)
```

You can increase node and edge sizes by specifying additional arguments.

```python
sc.pl.paga(adata, node_size_scale=10, edge_width_scale=2)
```

### Notes

When initializing the positions, note that – for some reason – igraph mirrors coordinates along the x axis… that is, you should increase the `maxiter` parameter by 1 if the layout is flipped.

#### SEE ALSO
`tl.paga`, `pl.paga_compare`, `pl.paga_path`

signature: scanpy.get.aggregate(adata, by, func, *, axis=None, mask=None, dof=1, layer=None, obsm=None, varm=None)
description:
Aggregate data matrix based on some categorical grouping.

This function is useful for pseudobulking as well as plotting.

Aggregation to perform is specified by `func`, which can be a single metric or a
list of metrics. Each metric is computed over the group and results in a new layer
in the output `AnnData` object.

If none of `layer`, `obsm`, or `varm` are passed in, `X` will be used for aggregation data.

params.name: adata
params.desc: to be aggregated.

params.name: by
params.desc: Key of the column to be grouped-by.

params.name: func
params.desc: How to aggregate.

params.name: axis
params.desc: Axis on which to find group by column.

params.name: mask
params.desc: Boolean mask (or key to column containing mask) to apply along the axis.

params.name: dof
params.desc: Degrees of freedom for variance. Defaults to 1.

params.name: layer
params.desc: If not None, key for aggregation data.

params.name: obsm
params.desc: If not None, key for aggregation data.

params.name: varm
params.desc: If not None, key for aggregation data.

params.name: return
params.desc: Aggregated AnnData.

misc: Examples show how to calculate mean expression and number of nonzero entries per cluster. Additionally, it shows grouping over multiple columns and notes that any combination of groups absent in the original data is filtered out.

signature: scanpy.tools.draw_graph(adata, layout='fa', *, init_pos=None, root=None, random_state=0, n_jobs=None, adjacency=None, key_added_ext=None, neighbors_key=None, obsp=None, copy=False, **kwds)
description: Force-directed graph drawing. An alternative to tSNE that often preserves the topology of the data better. This requires running `neighbors()` first.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: layout
params.desc: ‘fa’ (`ForceAtlas2`) or any valid igraph layout. Of particular interest are ‘fr’, ‘grid_fr’, ‘kk’, ‘lgl’, ‘drl’, and ‘rt’.

params.name: root
params.desc: Root for tree layouts.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: For layouts with random initialization like ‘fr’, change this to use different initial states for the optimization. If `None`, no seed is set.

params.name: adjacency
params.desc: Sparse adjacency matrix of the graph, defaults to neighbors connectivities.

params.name: key_added_ext
params.desc: By default, append `layout`.

params.name: init_pos
params.desc: ‘paga’/`True`, `None`/`False`, or any valid 2d-`.obsm` key. Use precomputed coordinates for initialization. If `False`/`None` (the default), initialize randomly.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: If not specified, draw_graph looks at .obsp['connectivities'] for connectivities. If specified, draw_graph looks at .obsp[.uns[neighbors_key]['connectivities_key']] for connectivities.

params.name: obsp
params.desc: Use .obsp[obsp] as adjacency. You can’t specify both `obsp` and `neighbors_key` at the same time.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Return a copy instead of writing to adata.

params.name: **kwds
params.desc: Parameters of chosen igraph layout. One of the most important ones is `maxiter`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.obsm['X_draw_graph_[layout | key_added_ext]']`: Coordinates of graph layout. `adata.uns['draw_graph']`: `draw_graph` parameters.

misc: Many layouts implemented in igraph are available. Requires running neighbors() first. The default layout uses `fa2-modified` package.

signature: scanpy.tools.embedding_density(adata, basis='umap', *, groupby=None, key_added=None, components=None)

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.highly_variable_genes(adata, *, layer=None, n_top_genes=None, min_disp=0.5, max_disp=inf, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, span=0.3, n_bins=20, flavor='seurat', subset=False, inplace=True, batch_key=None, check_values=True)
description: Annotate highly variable genes based on different methodologies including those from Seurat, Cell Ranger, and Seurat v3. Expects logarithmized data unless a specific flavor such as 'seurat_v3' is used where count data is expected.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: layer
params.desc: If provided, use `adata.layers[layer]` for expression values instead of `adata.X`.

params.name: n_top_genes
params.desc: Number of highly-variable genes to keep. Mandatory if `flavor='seurat_v3'`.

params.name: min_disp
params.desc: Minimum dispersion. If `n_top_genes` is not `None`, ignored. Ignored if `flavor='seurat_v3'`.

params.name: max_disp
params.desc: Maximum dispersion. If `n_top_genes` is not `None`, ignored. Ignored if `flavor='seurat_v3'`.

params.name: min_mean
params.desc: Minimum mean. If `n_top_genes` is not `None`, ignored. Ignored if `flavor='seurat_v3'`.

params.name: max_mean
params.desc: Maximum mean. If `n_top_genes` is not `None`, ignored. Ignored if `flavor='seurat_v3'`.

params.name: span
params.desc: The fraction of the data used when estimating the variance in the loess model fit if `flavor='seurat_v3'`.

params.name: n_bins
params.desc: Number of bins for binning the mean gene expression. Normalization is done with respect to each bin.

params.name: flavor
params.desc: Choose the flavor for identifying highly variable genes. Affects method of computation.

params.name: subset
params.desc: Inplace subset to highly-variable genes if `True` otherwise merely indicate highly variable genes.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Whether to place calculated metrics in `.var` or return them.

params.name: batch_key
params.desc: If specified, highly-variable genes are selected within each batch separately and merged.

params.name: check_values
params.desc: Check if counts in the selected layer are integers. Only used if `flavor='seurat_v3'`/`'seurat_v3_paper'`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns a `pandas.DataFrame` with calculated metrics if `inplace=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object with updated fields in `adata.var`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: groups
params.desc: The groups for which to show the gene ranking.

params.name: n_genes
params.desc: Number of genes to show. This can be a negative number to show for example the down regulated genes. eg: num_genes=-10. Is ignored if `gene_names` is passed.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider. By default, the groupby is chosen from the rank genes groups parameter but other groupby options can be used. It is expected that groupby is a categorical. If groupby is not a categorical observation, it would be subdivided into `num_categories`.

params.name: min_logfoldchange
params.desc: Value to filter genes in groups if their logfoldchange is less than the min_logfoldchange.

params.name: key
params.desc: Key used to store the ranking results in `adata.uns`.

params.name: values_to_plot

params.name: var_names
params.desc: Genes to plot. Sometimes is useful to pass a specific list of var names (e.g. genes) to check their fold changes or p-values, instead of the top/bottom genes. The var_names could be a dictionary or a list.
