misc:
### Examples

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(adata, 'bulk_labels', n_genes=adata.raw.shape[1])
```

Plot `logfoldchanges` instead of gene expression. In this case a diverging colormap like `bwr` or `seismic` works better. To center the colormap in zero, the minimum and maximum values to plot are set to -4 and 4 respectively. Also, only genes with a log fold change of 3 or more are shown.

```python
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_matrixplot(
    adata,
    n_genes=4,
    values_to_plot="logfoldchanges",
    cmap='bwr',
    vmin=-4,
    vmax=4,
    min_logfoldchange=3,
    colorbar_title='log fold change',
)
```

Also, the last genes can be plotted. This can be useful to identify genes that are lowly expressed in a group. For this `n_genes=-4` is used.

```python
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_matrixplot(
    adata,
    n_genes=-4,
    values_to_plot="logfoldchanges",
    cmap='bwr',
    vmin=-4,
    vmax=4,
    min_logfoldchange=3,
    colorbar_title='log fold change',
)
```

A list specific genes can be given to check their log fold change. If a dictionary, the dictionary keys will be added as labels in the plot.

```python
var_names = {"T-cell": ['CD3D', 'CD3E', 'IL32'],
              'B-cell': ['CD79A', 'CD79B', 'MS4A1'],
              'myeloid': ['CST3', 'LYZ'] }
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_matrixplot(
    adata,
    var_names=var_names,
    values_to_plot="logfoldchanges",
    cmap='bwr',
    vmin=-4,
    vmax=4,
    min_logfoldchange=3,
    colorbar_title='log fold change',
)
```

signature: scanpy.plotting.heatmap(adata, var_names, groupby, *, use_raw=None, log=False, num_categories=7, dendrogram=False, gene_symbols=None, var_group_positions=None, var_group_labels=None, var_group_rotation=None, layer=None, standard_scale=None, swap_axes=False, show_gene_labels=None, show=None, save=None, figsize=None, vmin=None, vmax=None, vcenter=None, norm=None, **kwds)
description:
Heatmap of the expression values of genes.

If `groupby` is given, the heatmap is ordered by the respective group. For example, a list of marker genes can be plotted, ordered by clustering. If the `groupby` observation annotation is not categorical the observation annotation is turned into a categorical by binning the data into the number specified in `num_categories`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: var_names
params.desc: `var_names` should be a valid subset of `adata.var_names`. If `var_names` is a mapping, then the key is used as label to group the values (see `var_group_labels`). The mapping values should be sequences of valid `adata.var_names`. In this case either coloring or ‘brackets’ are used for the grouping of var names depending on the plot. When `var_names` is a mapping, then the `var_group_labels` and `var_group_positions` are set.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present.

params.name: log
params.desc: Plot on logarithmic axis.

params.name: num_categories
params.desc: Only used if groupby observation is not categorical. This value determines the number of groups into which the groupby observation should be subdivided.

params.name: categories_order
params.desc: Order in which to show the categories. Note: add_dendrogram or add_totals can change the categories order.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Figure size when `multi_panel=True`. Otherwise the `rcParam['figure.figsize]` value is used. Format is (width, height)

params.name: dendrogram
params.desc: If True or a valid dendrogram key, a dendrogram based on the hierarchical clustering between the `groupby` categories is added. The dendrogram information is computed using `scanpy.tl.dendrogram()`. If `tl.dendrogram` has not been called previously the function is called with default parameters.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: var_group_positions
params.desc: Use this parameter to highlight groups of `var_names`. This will draw a ‘bracket’ or a color block between the given start and end positions. If the parameter `var_group_labels` is set, the corresponding labels are added on top/left. E.g. `var_group_positions=[(4,10)]` will add a bracket between the fourth `var_name` and the tenth `var_name`. By giving more positions, more brackets/color blocks are drawn.

params.name: var_group_labels
params.desc: Labels for each of the `var_group_positions` that want to be highlighted.

params.name: var_group_rotation
params.desc: Label rotation degrees. By default, labels larger than 4 characters are rotated 90 degrees.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Name of the AnnData object layer that wants to be plotted. By default adata.raw.X is plotted. If `use_raw=False` is set, then `adata.X` is plotted. If `layer` is set to a valid layer name, then the layer is plotted. `layer` takes precedence over `use_raw`.

params.name: standard_scale
params.desc: Whether or not to standardize that dimension between 0 and 1, meaning for each variable or observation, subtract the minimum and divide each by its maximum.

params.name: swap_axes
params.desc: By default, the x axis contains `var_names` (e.g. genes) and the y axis the `groupby` categories (if any). By setting `swap_axes` then x are the `groupby` categories and y the `var_names`.

params.name: show_gene_labels
params.desc: By default gene labels are shown when there are 50 or less genes. Otherwise the labels are removed.

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: vmin
params.desc: The value representing the lower limit of the color scale. Values smaller than vmin are plotted with the same color as vmin.

params.name: vmax
params.desc: The value representing the upper limit of the color scale. Values larger than vmax are plotted with the same color as vmax.

params.name: vcenter
params.desc: The value representing the center of the color scale. Useful for diverging colormaps.

params.name: norm
params.desc: Custom color normalization object from matplotlib. See https://matplotlib.org/stable/tutorials/colors/colormapnorms.html for details.

params.name: **kwds
params.desc: Are passed to `matplotlib.pyplot.imshow()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Dict of `Axes`.

misc:
### Examples

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
markers = ['C1QA', 'PSAP', 'CD79A', 'CD79B', 'CST3', 'LYZ']
sc.pl.heatmap(adata, markers, groupby='bulk_labels', swap_axes=True)
```

#### SEE ALSO
`pl.rank_genes_groups_heatmap`, `tl.rank_genes_groups`

signature: scanpy.tools.umap(adata, *, min_dist=0.5, spread=1.0, n_components=2, maxiter=None, alpha=1.0, gamma=1.0, negative_sample_rate=5, init_pos='spectral', random_state=0, a=None, b=None, method='umap', key_added=None, neighbors_key='neighbors', copy=False)
description: Embed the neighborhood graph using UMAP. UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) is a manifold learning technique suitable for visualizing high-dimensional data. Besides tending to be faster than tSNE, it optimizes the embedding such that it best reflects the topology of the data, which we represent throughout Scanpy using a neighborhood graph. tSNE, by contrast, optimizes the distribution of nearest-neighbor distances in the embedding such that these best match the distribution of distances in the high-dimensional space. We use the implementation of umap-learn.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: min_dist
params.desc: The effective minimum distance between embedded points. Smaller values will result in a more clustered/clumped embedding where nearby points on the manifold are drawn closer together, while larger values will result on a more even dispersal of points. The value should be set relative to the `spread` value, which determines the scale at which embedded points will be spread out.

params.name: spread
params.desc: The effective scale of embedded points. In combination with `min_dist` this determines how clustered/clumped the embedded points are.

params.name: n_components
params.desc: The number of dimensions of the embedding.

params.name: maxiter
params.desc: The number of iterations (epochs) of the optimization. Called `n_epochs` in the original UMAP.

params.name: alpha
params.desc: The initial learning rate for the embedding optimization.

params.name: gamma
params.desc: Weighting applied to negative samples in low dimensional embedding optimization. Values higher than one will result in greater weight being given to negative samples.

params.name: negative_sample_rate
params.desc: The number of negative edge/1-simplex samples to use per positive edge/1-simplex sample in optimizing the low dimensional embedding.

params.name: init_pos
params.desc: How to initialize the low dimensional embedding. Called `init` in the original UMAP. Options are: Any key for `adata.obsm`, 'paga', 'spectral', 'random', or a numpy array of initial embedding positions.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Seed used by the random number generator; or the random number generator itself.

params.name: a
params.desc: More specific parameters controlling the embedding. If `None` these values are set automatically as determined by `min_dist` and `spread`.

params.name: b
params.desc: More specific parameters controlling the embedding. If `None` these values are set automatically as determined by `min_dist` and `spread`.

params.name: method
params.desc: Chosen implementation. Options are 'umap' for Umap’s simplical set embedding or 'rapids' for GPU accelerated implementation.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: If not specified, the embedding is stored as `obsm['X_umap']` and the parameters in `uns['umap']`. If specified, the embedding is stored as `obsm[key_added]` and the parameters in `uns[key_added]`.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: Umap looks in `uns[neighbors_key]` for neighbors settings and `obsp[.uns[neighbors_key]['connectivities_key']]` for connectivities.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Return a copy instead of writing to `adata`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.obsm['X_umap' | key_added]` for UMAP coordinates of data, `adata.uns['umap' | key_added]` for UMAP parameters.

`rank_genes_groups_matrixplot()`

### Examples

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
markers = ['C1QA', 'PSAP', 'CD79A', 'CD79B', 'CST3', 'LYZ']
```

Using var_names as dict:

```python

```

Get Matrix object for fine tuning:

```python
mp = sc.pl.matrixplot(adata, markers, 'bulk_labels', return_fig=True)
mp.add_totals().style(edge_color='black').show()
```

The axes used can be obtained using the get_axes() method

```python
axes_dict = mp.get_axes()
```

signature: scanpy.tools.marker_gene_overlap(adata, reference_markers, *, key='rank_genes_groups', method='overlap_count', normalize=None, top_n_markers=None, adj_pval_threshold=None, key_added='marker_gene_overlap', inplace=False)
description: Calculate an overlap score between data-derived marker genes and provided markers. Marker gene overlap scores can be quoted as overlap counts, overlap coefficients, or jaccard indices. The method returns a pandas dataframe which can be used to annotate clusters based on marker gene overlaps.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix.

params.name: reference_markers
params.desc: A marker gene dictionary object. Keys should be strings with the cell identity name and values are sets or lists of strings which match format of `adata.var_name`.

params.name: key
params.desc: The key in `adata.uns` where the rank_genes_groups output is stored. By default this is `'rank_genes_groups'`.

params.name: method
params.desc: (default: `overlap_count`) Method to calculate marker gene overlap. `'overlap_count'` uses the intersection of the gene set, `'overlap_coef'` uses the overlap coefficient, and `'jaccard'` uses the Jaccard index.

params.name: normalize
params.desc: Normalization option for the marker gene overlap output. This parameter can only be set when `method` is set to `'overlap_count'`. `'reference'` normalizes the data by the total number of marker genes given in the reference annotation per group. `'data'` normalizes the data by the total number of marker genes used for each cluster.

params.name: top_n_markers
params.desc: The number of top data-derived marker genes to use. By default the top 100 marker genes are used. If `adj_pval_threshold` is set along with `top_n_markers`, then `adj_pval_threshold` is ignored.

params.name: adj_pval_threshold
params.desc: A significance threshold on the adjusted p-values to select marker genes. This can only be used when adjusted p-values are calculated by `sc.tl.rank_genes_groups()`. If `adj_pval_threshold` is set along with `top_n_markers`, then `adj_pval_threshold` is ignored.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Name of the `.uns` field that will contain the marker overlap scores.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Return a marker gene dataframe or store it inplace in `adata.uns`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `pandas.DataFrame` if `inplace=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object where it sets the following field: `adata.uns[key_added]`: Marker gene overlap scores. Default for `key_added` is `'marker_gene_overlap'`.

misc: This function was written by Malte Luecken.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.filter_genes(data, *, min_counts=None, min_cells=None, max_counts=None, max_cells=None, inplace=True, copy=False)
description:
Filter genes based on number of cells or counts. Keep genes that have at least `min_counts` counts or are expressed in at
least `min_cells` cells or have at most `max_counts` counts or are expressed
in at most `max_cells` cells. Only provide one of the optional parameters `min_counts`, `min_cells`,
`max_counts`, `max_cells` per call.

params.name: data
params.desc: An annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: min_counts
params.desc: Minimum number of counts required for a gene to pass filtering.

params.name: min_cells
params.desc: Minimum number of cells expressed required for a gene to pass filtering.

params.name: max_counts
params.desc: Maximum number of counts required for a gene to pass filtering.

params.name: max_cells
params.desc: Maximum number of cells expressed required for a gene to pass filtering.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Perform computation inplace or return result.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If `True`, a copy of the object is made, otherwise changes are done in place.

params.name: return
params.desc: Depending on `inplace`, returns the following arrays or directly subsets and annotates the data matrix.

misc:
Returns
- **gene_subset** : Boolean index mask that does filtering. `True` means that the gene is kept. `False` means the gene is removed.
- **number_per_gene** : Depending on what was thresholded (`counts` or `cells`), the array stores `n_counts` or `n_cells` per gene.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.subsample(data, fraction=None, *, n_obs=None, random_state=0, copy=False)
description: Subsample to a fraction of the number of observations.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: fraction
params.desc: Subsample to this `fraction` of the number of observations.

params.name: n_obs
params.desc: Subsample to this number of observations.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Random seed to change subsampling.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If an `AnnData` is passed, determines whether a copy is returned.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `X[obs_indices], obs_indices` if data is array-like, otherwise subsamples the passed `AnnData` (`copy == False`) or returns a subsampled copy of it (`copy == True`).

signature: scanpy.tools.paga(adata, groups=None, *, use_rna_velocity=False, model='v1.2', neighbors_key=None, copy=False)
description: Map out the coarse-grained connectivity structures of complex manifolds. By quantifying the connectivity of partitions (groups, clusters) of the single-cell graph, partition-based graph abstraction (PAGA) generates a much simpler abstracted graph (PAGA graph) of partitions, in which edge weights represent confidence in the presence of connections. By thresholding this confidence in `paga()`, a much simpler representation of the manifold data is obtained, which is nonetheless faithful to the topology of the manifold.

params.name: adata
params.desc: An annotated data matrix.

params.name: groups
params.desc: Key for categorical in `adata.obs`. You can pass your predefined groups by choosing any categorical annotation of observations. Default: The first present key of `'leiden'` or `'louvain'`.

params.name: use_rna_velocity
params.desc: Use RNA velocity to orient edges in the abstracted graph and estimate transitions. Requires that `adata.uns` contains a directed single-cell graph with key `['velocity_graph']`. This feature might be subject to change in the future.

params.name: model
params.desc: The PAGA connectivity model.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: If not specified, paga looks `.uns['neighbors']` for neighbors settings and `.obsp['connectivities']`, `.obsp['distances']` for connectivities and distances respectively (default storage places for `pp.neighbors`). If specified, paga looks `.uns[neighbors_key]` for neighbors settings and `.obsp[.uns[neighbors_key]['connectivities_key']]`, `.obsp[.uns[neighbors_key]['distances_key']]` for connectivities and distances respectively.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Copy `adata` before computation and return a copy. Otherwise, perform computation inplace and return `None`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.uns['connectivities']` and `adata.uns['connectivities_tree']`.

misc:
Together with a random walk-based distance measure (e.g. `scanpy.tl.dpt()`) this generates a partial coordinatization of data useful for exploring and explaining its variation. 

#### SEE ALSO
`pl.paga`, `pl.paga_path`, `pl.paga_compare`

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.calculate_qc_metrics(adata, *, expr_type='counts', var_type='genes', qc_vars=(), percent_top=(50, 100, 200, 500), layer=None, use_raw=False, inplace=False, log1p=True, parallel=None)
description: Calculate quality control metrics for an AnnData object, including various observation and variable level metrics based on expression data.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: expr_type
params.desc: Name of kind of values in X.

params.name: var_type
params.desc: The kind of thing the variables are.

params.name: qc_vars
params.desc: Keys for boolean columns of `.var` which identify variables you might want to control for (e.g. 'ERCC' or 'mito').

params.name: percent_top
params.desc: List of ranks at which cumulative expression proportion is reported as a percentage to assess library complexity.

params.name: layer
params.desc: If provided, use `adata.layers[layer]` for expression values instead of `adata.X`.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: If True, use `adata.raw.X` for expression values instead of `adata.X`.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Whether to place calculated metrics in `adata`’s `.obs` and `.var`.

params.name: log1p
params.desc: Set to `False` to skip computing `log1p` transformed annotations.

params.name: parallel
params.desc: Unused parameter.

params.name: return
params.desc: Depending on `inplace`, returns calculated metrics as DataFrames or updates `adata`’s `obs` and `var`.

misc: Metrics such as total counts, percentages, and means are calculated for observation and variable levels. Annotations can be added to `adata` directly if `inplace=True`.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.normalize_total(adata, *, target_sum=None, exclude_highly_expressed=False, max_fraction=0.05, key_added=None, layer=None, layers=None, layer_norm=None, inplace=True, copy=False)
description:
Normalize counts per cell.

Normalize each cell by total counts over all genes,
so that every cell has the same total count after normalization.
If choosing `target_sum=1e6`, this is CPM normalization.

If `exclude_highly_expressed=True`, very highly expressed genes are excluded
from the computation of the normalization factor (size factor) for each
cell. This is meaningful as these can strongly influence the resulting
normalized values for all other genes.

Similar functions are used by Seurat, Cell Ranger, or SPRING.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: target_sum
params.desc: If `None`, after normalization, each observation (cell) has a total count equal to the median of total counts for observations (cells) before normalization.

params.name: exclude_highly_expressed
params.desc: Exclude (very) highly expressed genes for the computation of the normalization factor (size factor) for each cell. A gene is considered highly expressed, if it has more than `max_fraction` of the total counts in at least one cell.

params.name: max_fraction
params.desc: If `exclude_highly_expressed=True`, consider cells as highly expressed that have more counts than `max_fraction` of the original total counts in at least one cell.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Name of the field in `adata.obs` where the normalization factor is stored.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Layer to normalize instead of `X`. If `None`, `X` is normalized.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Whether to update `adata` or return dictionary with normalized copies of `adata.X` and `adata.layers`.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Whether to modify copied input object. Not compatible with inplace=False.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns dictionary with normalized copies of `adata.X` and `adata.layers` or updates `adata` with normalized version of the original `adata.X` and `adata.layers`, depending on `inplace`.

misc:
### Example

```pycon
>>> import sys
>>> from anndata import AnnData
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> sc.settings.verbosity = "info"
>>> sc.settings.logfile = sys.stdout  # for doctests
>>> np.set_printoptions(precision=2)
>>> adata = AnnData(
...     np.array(
...         [
...             [3, 3, 3, 6, 6],
...             [1, 1, 1, 2, 2],
...             [1, 22, 1, 2, 2],
...         ],
...         dtype="float32",
...     )
... )
>>> adata.X
array([[ 3.,  3.,  3.,  6.,  6.],
       [ 1.,  1.,  1.,  2.,  2.],
       [ 1., 22.,  1.,  2.,  2.]], dtype=float32)
>>> X_norm = sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1, inplace=False)["X"]
normalizing counts per cell
    finished (0:00:00)
>>> X_norm
array([[0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.29, 0.29],
       [0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.29, 0.29],
       [0.04, 0.79, 0.04, 0.07, 0.07]], dtype=float32)
>>> X_norm = sc.pp.normalize_total(
...     adata,
...     target_sum=1,
...     exclude_highly_expressed=True,
...     max_fraction=0.2,
...     inplace=False,
... )["X"]
normalizing counts per cell. The following highly-expressed genes are not considered during normalization factor computation:
['1', '3', '4']
    finished (0:00:00)
>>> X_norm
array([[ 0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  1. ,  1. ],
       [ 0.5,  0.5,  0.5,  1. ,  1. ],
       [ 0.5, 11. ,  0.5,  1. ,  1. ]], dtype=float32)
```

signature: scanpy.plotting.tracksplot(adata, var_names, groupby, *, use_raw=None, log=False, dendrogram=False, gene_symbols=None, var_group_positions=None, var_group_labels=None, layer=None, show=None, save=None, figsize=None, **kwds)
description: Compact plot of expression of a list of genes. In this type of plot each var_name is plotted as a filled line plot where the y values correspond to the var_name values and x is each of the cells. Best results are obtained when using raw counts that are not log. `groupby` is required to sort and order the values using the respective group and should be a categorical value.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: var_names
params.desc: `var_names` should be a valid subset of `adata.var_names`. If `var_names` is a mapping, then the key is used as label to group the values (see `var_group_labels`). The mapping values should be sequences of valid `adata.var_names`. In this case either coloring or ‘brackets’ are used for the grouping of var names depending on the plot. When `var_names` is a mapping, then the `var_group_labels` and `var_group_positions` are set.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present.

params.name: log
params.desc: Plot on logarithmic axis.

params.name: dendrogram
params.desc: If True or a valid dendrogram key, a dendrogram based on the hierarchical clustering between the `groupby` categories is added. The dendrogram information is computed using `scanpy.tl.dendrogram()`. If `tl.dendrogram` has not been called previously the function is called with default parameters.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: var_group_positions
params.desc: Use this parameter to highlight groups of `var_names`. This will draw a ‘bracket’ or a color block between the given start and end positions. If the parameter `var_group_labels` is set, the corresponding labels are added on top/left. E.g. `var_group_positions=[(4,10)]` will add a bracket between the fourth `var_name` and the tenth `var_name`. By giving more positions, more brackets/color blocks are drawn.

params.name: var_group_labels
params.desc: Labels for each of the `var_group_positions` that want to be highlighted.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Name of the AnnData object layer that wants to be plotted. By default adata.raw.X is plotted. If `use_raw=False` is set, then `adata.X` is plotted. If `layer` is set to a valid layer name, then the layer is plotted. `layer` takes precedence over `use_raw`.

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: **kwds
params.desc: Are passed to `heatmap()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: A dictionary with keys as plot component names and values as matplotlib Axes objects.

misc:
### Examples

Using var_names as list:

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
markers = ['C1QA', 'PSAP', 'CD79A', 'CD79B', 'CST3', 'LYZ']
sc.pl.tracksplot(adata, markers, groupby='bulk_labels', dendrogram=True)
```

Using var_names as dict:

```python
markers = {'T-cell': 'CD3D', 'B-cell': 'CD79A', 'myeloid': 'CST3'}
sc.pl.tracksplot(adata, markers, groupby='bulk_labels', dendrogram=True)
```

signature: scanpy.tools.score_genes_cell_cycle(adata, *, s_genes, g2m_genes, copy=False, **kwargs)
description: Given two lists of genes associated to S phase and G2M phase, calculates scores and assigns a cell cycle phase (G1, S or G2M).

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix.

params.name: s_genes
params.desc: List of genes associated with S phase.

params.name: g2m_genes
params.desc: List of genes associated with G2M phase.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Copy `adata` or modify it inplace.

params.name: **kwargs
params.desc: Are passed to `score_genes()`. `ctrl_size` is not possible, as it’s set as `min(len(s_genes), len(g2m_genes))`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object.

misc:
Sets the following fields:

`adata.obs['S_score']`
: The score for S phase for each cell.

`adata.obs['G2M_score']`
: The score for G2M phase for each cell.

`adata.obs['phase']`
: The cell cycle phase (`S`, `G2M` or `G1`) for each cell.

### SEE ALSO
`score_genes`

signature: scanpy.tools.filter_rank_genes_groups(adata, *, key=None, groupby=None, use_raw=None, key_added='rank_genes_groups_filtered', min_in_group_fraction=0.25, min_fold_change=1, max_out_group_fraction=0.5, compare_abs=False)
description: Filter out genes based on two criteria: log fold change and fraction of genes expressing the gene within and outside the `groupby` categories. Results are stored in `adata.uns[key_added]` (default: ‘rank_genes_groups_filtered’). To preserve the original structure of adata.uns[‘rank_genes_groups’], filtered genes are set to `NaN`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: key
params.desc: Key from `adata.uns` where results from `rank_genes_groups` are stored.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key from `adata.obs` to group the data.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key under which to add the filtered result to `adata.uns`.

params.name: min_in_group_fraction
params.desc: Minimum fraction of cells required to express a gene in the group.

params.name: min_fold_change
params.desc: Minimum log fold change required for inclusion in the results.

params.name: max_out_group_fraction
params.desc: Maximum fraction of cells expressing the gene outside of the group.

params.name: compare_abs
params.desc: If `True`, compare absolute values of log fold change with `min_fold_change`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Same output as `scanpy.tl.rank_genes_groups()` but with filtered genes names set to `NaN`.

misc:
### Examples
```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
>>> sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(adata, "bulk_labels", method="wilcoxon")
>>> sc.tl.filter_rank_genes_groups(adata, min_fold_change=3)
>>> # visualize results
>>> sc.pl.rank_genes_groups(adata, key="rank_genes_groups_filtered")
>>> # visualize results using dotplot
>>> sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(adata, key="rank_genes_groups_filtered")
```

signature: scanpy.metrics.morans_i(adata, *, vals=None, use_graph=None, layer=None, obsm=None, obsp=None, use_raw=False)
description: Calculate Moran’s I Global Autocorrelation Statistic. Moran’s I is a global autocorrelation statistic for some measure on a graph. It is commonly used in spatial data analysis to assess autocorrelation on a 2D grid. It is closely related to Geary’s C, but not identical.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Input data matrix.

params.name: vals
params.desc: Values to calculate Moran’s I for. Can be a 1D array or 2D matrix.

params.name: use_graph
params.desc: Key to use for graph in anndata object. If not provided, default neighbors connectivities will be used.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Key for `adata.layers` to choose `vals`.

params.name: obsm
params.desc: Key for `adata.obsm` to choose `vals`.

params.name: obsp
params.desc: Key for `adata.obsp` to choose `vals`.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to use `adata.raw.X` for `vals`.

params.name: g
params.desc: The graph, if calling the function directly on the graph and values.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns a 1D ndarray if `vals` is 2D, otherwise returns a scalar if `vals` is 1D.

misc:
### Examples

Calculate Moran’s I for each components of a dimensionality reduction:
```python
import scanpy as sc, numpy as np

pbmc = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_processed()
pc_c = sc.metrics.morans_i(pbmc, obsm="X_pca")
```

It’s equivalent to call the function directly on the underlying arrays:
```python
alt = sc.metrics.morans_i(pbmc.obsp["connectivities"], pbmc.obsm["X_pca"].T)
np.testing.assert_array_equal(pc_c, alt)
```

description: Scatter plot in UMAP basis.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: color
params.desc: Keys for annotations of observations/cells or variables/genes, e.g., `'ann1'` or `['ann1', 'ann2']`.

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `.raw` attribute of `adata` for coloring with gene expression. If `None`, defaults to `True` if `layer` isn’t provided and `adata.raw` is present.

params.name: layer
params.type: Name of the AnnData object layer
params.desc: Name of the AnnData object layer that wants to be plotted. By default adata.raw.X is plotted. If `use_raw=False` is set, then `adata.X` is plotted. If `layer` is set to a valid layer name, then the layer is plotted. `layer` takes precedence over `use_raw`.

params.name: edges
params.desc: Show edges.

params.name: edges_width
params.desc: Width of edges.

params.name: edges_color
params.desc: Color of edges.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: Where to look for neighbors connectivities. If not specified, this looks .obsp[‘connectivities’] for connectivities (default storage place for pp.neighbors). If specified, this looks .obsp[.uns[neighbors_key][‘connectivities_key’]] for connectivities.

params.name: arrows
params.desc: Show arrows (deprecated in favour of `scvelo.pl.velocity_embedding`).

params.name: arrows_kwds
params.desc: Passed to `quiver()`.

params.name: sort_order
params.desc: For continuous annotations used as color parameter, plot data points with higher values on top of others.

params.name: groups
params.desc: Restrict to a few categories in categorical observation annotation. The default is not to restrict to any groups.

params.name: dimensions
params.desc: 0-indexed dimensions of the embedding to plot as integers. E.g. [(0, 1), (1, 2)]. Unlike `components`, this argument is used in the same way as `colors`, e.g. is used to specify a single plot at a time. Will eventually replace the components argument.

params.name: components
params.desc: For instance, `['1,2', '2,3']`. To plot all available components use `components='all'`.

params.name: projection
params.desc: Projection of plot (default: `'2d'`).

params.name: legend_loc
params.desc: Location of legend, either `'on data'`, `'right margin'`, `None`, or a valid keyword for the `loc` parameter of `Legend`.

params.name: legend_fontsize
params.desc: Numeric size in pt or string describing the size. See `set_fontsize()`.

params.name: legend_fontweight
params.desc: Legend font weight. A numeric value in range 0-1000 or a string. Defaults to `'bold'` if `legend_loc == 'on data'`, otherwise to `'normal'`. See `set_fontweight()`.

params.name: legend_fontoutline
params.desc: Line width of the legend font outline in pt. Draws a white outline using the path effect `withStroke`.

params.name: colorbar_loc
params.desc: Where to place the colorbar for continuous variables. If `None`, no colorbar is added.

params.name: size
params.desc: Point size. If `None`, is automatically computed as 120000 / n_cells. Can be a sequence containing the size for each cell. The order should be the same as in adata.obs.

params.name: color_map
params.desc: Color map to use for continuous variables. Can be a name or a `Colormap` instance.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Colors to use for plotting categorical annotation groups. The palette can be a valid `ListedColormap` name, a `Cycler` object, a dict mapping categories to colors, or a sequence of colors.

params.name: na_color
params.desc: Color to use for null or masked values. Can be anything matplotlib accepts as a color. Used for all points if `color=None`.

params.name: na_in_legend
params.desc: If there are missing values, whether they get an entry in the legend. Currently only implemented for categorical legends.

params.name: frameon
params.desc: Draw a frame around the scatter plot. Defaults to value set in `set_figure_params()`, defaults to `True`.

params.name: title
params.desc: Provide title for panels either as string or list of strings, e.g. `['title1', 'title2', ...]`.

params.name: vmin
params.desc: The value representing the lower limit of the color scale. Values smaller than vmin are plotted with the same color as vmin. vmin can be a number, a string, a function or `None`. If it is a string with the format `pN`, it is interpreted as a vmin=percentile(N).

params.name: vmax
params.desc: The value representing the upper limit of the color scale. The format is the same as for `vmin`.

params.name: vcenter
params.desc: The value representing the center of the color scale. Useful for diverging colormaps.

params.name: add_outline
params.desc: If set to True, this will add a thin border around groups of dots. In some situations this can enhance the aesthetics of the resulting image.

params.name: outline_color
params.desc: Tuple with two valid color names used to adjust the add_outline. The first color is the border color (default: black), while the second color is a gap color between the border color and the scatter dot (default: white).

params.name: outline_width
params.desc: Tuple with two width numbers used to adjust the outline. The first value is the width of the border color as a fraction of the scatter dot size (default: 0.3). The second value is width of the gap color (default: 0.05).

params.name: ncols
params.desc: Number of panels per row.

params.name: wspace
params.desc: Adjust the width of the space between multiple panels.

params.name: hspace
params.desc: Adjust the height of the space between multiple panels.

params.name: return_fig
params.desc: Return the matplotlib figure.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Arguments to pass to `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()`, for instance: the maximum and minimum values.

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: return
params.desc: If `show==False` a `Axes` or a list of it.

signature: scanpy.plotting.violin(adata, keys, groupby=None, *, log=False, use_raw=None, stripplot=True, jitter=True, size=1, layer=None, density_norm='width', order=None, multi_panel=None, xlabel='', ylabel=None, rotation=None, show=None, save=None, ax=None, scale=_empty, **kwds)
description:
Violin plot.

Wraps seaborn.violinplot for AnnData.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: keys
params.desc: Keys for accessing variables of `.var_names` or fields of `.obs`.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider.

params.name: log
params.desc: Plot on logarithmic axis.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to use `raw` attribute of `adata`. Defaults to `True` if `.raw` is present.

params.name: stripplot
params.desc: Add a stripplot on top of the violin plot.

params.name: jitter
params.desc: Add jitter to the stripplot (only when stripplot is True).

params.name: size
params.desc: Size of the jitter points.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Name of the AnnData object layer to be plotted. By default `adata.raw.X` is plotted.

params.name: density_norm
params.desc: The method used to scale the width of each violin.

params.name: order
params.desc: Order in which to show the categories.

params.name: multi_panel
params.desc: Display keys in multiple panels also when `groupby is not None`.

params.name: xlabel
params.desc: Label of the x axis.

params.name: ylabel
params.desc: Label of the y axis.

params.name: rotation
params.desc: Rotation of xtick labels.

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: **kwds
params.desc: Are passed to `violinplot()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: A `Axes` object if `ax` is `None` else `None`.

misc:
### Examples

```python
import scanpy as sc
adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
sc.pl.violin(adata, keys='S_score')
```

Plot by category. Rotate x-axis labels so that they do not overlap.

```python
sc.pl.violin(adata, keys='S_score', groupby='bulk_labels', rotation=90)
```

Set order of categories to be plotted or select specific categories to be plotted.

```python
groupby_order = ['CD34+', 'CD19+ B']
sc.pl.violin(adata, keys='S_score', groupby='bulk_labels', rotation=90,
    order=groupby_order)
```

Plot multiple keys.

```python
sc.pl.violin(adata, keys=['S_score', 'G2M_score'], groupby='bulk_labels',
    rotation=90)
```

For large datasets consider omitting the overlaid scatter plot.

```python
sc.pl.violin(adata, keys='S_score', stripplot=False)
```

signature: scanpy.tools.louvain(adata, resolution=None, *, random_state=0, restrict_to=None, key_added='louvain', adjacency=None, flavor='vtraag', directed=True, use_weights=False, partition_type=None, partition_kwargs=mappingproxy({}), neighbors_key=None, obsp=None, copy=False)
description: Cluster cells into subgroups using the Louvain algorithm. This method is designed for single-cell analysis and requires either prior execution of `neighbors()` or explicit provision of an `adjacency` matrix.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix.

params.name: resolution
params.desc: Resolution parameter for finding clusters, applicable for 'vtraag' and 'RAPIDS' flavors. Defaults to 1.0.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Initialization setting for optimization.

params.name: restrict_to
params.desc: Tuple that limits clustering to certain categories within the key for sample annotation.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key under which to add the cluster labels. Default is 'louvain'.

params.name: adjacency
params.desc: Graph's adjacency matrix, defaulting to neighbors connectivities.

params.name: flavor
params.desc: Package choice for clustering; options are 'vtraag', 'igraph', and 'rapids'. The default is 'vtraag'. The 'rapids' option is deprecated since version 1.10.0.

params.name: directed
params.desc: Whether to interpret the adjacency matrix as a directed graph.

params.name: use_weights
params.desc: Indicates whether to use weights from the knn graph.

params.name: partition_type
params.desc: Partition type used, valid only if flavor is 'vtraag'.

params.name: partition_kwargs
params.desc: Arguments for partitioning passed to 'vtraag' method.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: Key to use neighbors connectivities as the adjacency.

params.name: obsp
params.desc: Uses '.obsp[obsp]' as adjacency. Cannot be used together with neighbors_key.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Determines whether to modify `adata` in-place or return a copy.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object with added cluster labels.

misc: This function must be executed after creating a neighborhood graph or by passing an adjacency matrix directly.

signature: scanpy.tools.score_genes(adata, gene_list, *, ctrl_as_ref=True, ctrl_size=50, gene_pool=None, n_bins=25, score_name='score', random_state=0, copy=False, use_raw=None, layer=None)
description: Score a set of genes. The score is the average expression of a set of genes after subtraction by the average expression of a reference set of genes. The reference set is randomly sampled from the `gene_pool` for each binned expression value.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix.

params.name: gene_list
params.desc: The list of gene names used for score calculation.

params.name: ctrl_as_ref
params.desc: Allow the algorithm to use the control genes as reference. Will be changed to `False` in scanpy 2.0.

params.name: ctrl_size
params.desc: Number of reference genes to be sampled from each bin. If `len(gene_list)` is not too low, you can set `ctrl_size=len(gene_list)`.

params.name: gene_pool
params.desc: Genes for sampling the reference set. Default is all genes.

params.name: n_bins
params.desc: Number of expression level bins for sampling.

params.name: score_name
params.desc: Name of the field to be added in `.obs`.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: The random seed for sampling.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Copy `adata` or modify it inplace.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Whether to use `raw` attribute of `adata`. Defaults to `True` if `.raw` is present.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Key from `adata.layers` whose value will be used to perform tests on.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following field: `adata.obs[score_name]`: Scores of each cell.

misc: Examples can be found in the `Scanpy` usage notebooks.

signature: scanpy.concat(adatas, *, axis='obs', join='inner', merge=None, uns_merge=None, label=None, keys=None, index_unique=None, fill_value=None, pairwise=False)
description: Concatenates AnnData objects along an axis.

params.name: adatas
params.desc: The objects to be concatenated. If a Mapping is passed, keys are used for the `keys` argument and values are concatenated.

params.name: axis
params.desc: Which axis to concatenate along.

params.name: join
params.desc: How to align values when concatenating. If “outer”, the union of the other axis is taken. If “inner”, the intersection.

params.name: merge
params.desc: How elements not aligned to the axis being concatenated along are selected.

params.name: uns_merge
params.desc: How the elements of `.uns` are selected. Uses the same set of strategies as the `merge` argument, except applied recursively.

params.name: label
params.desc: Column in axis annotation (i.e. `.obs` or `.var`) to place batch information in. If it’s None, no column is added.

params.name: keys
params.desc: Names for each object being added. These values are used for column values for `label` or appended to the index if `index_unique` is not `None`.

params.name: index_unique
params.desc: Whether to make the index unique by using the keys. If provided, this is the delimiter between “{orig_idx}{index_unique}{key}”.

params.name: fill_value
params.desc: When `join="outer"`, this is the value that will be used to fill the introduced indices.

params.name: pairwise
params.desc: Whether pairwise elements along the concatenated dimension should be included.

params.name: return
params.desc: The concatenated AnnData object.

misc:
### Notes

#### WARNING
If you use `join='outer'` this fills 0s for sparse data when variables are absent in a batch. Use this with care. Dense data is filled with `NaN`.

#### Examples

Preparing example objects and usage patterns.

signature: scanpy.plotting.StackedViolin(adata, var_names, groupby, *, use_raw=None, log=False, num_categories=7, categories_order=None, title=None, figsize=None, gene_symbols=None, var_group_positions=None, var_group_labels=None, var_group_rotation=None, layer=None, standard_scale=None, ax=None, vmin=None, vmax=None, vcenter=None, norm=None, **kwds)
description:
Stacked violin plots.

Makes a compact image composed of individual violin plots stacked on top of each other. Useful to visualize gene expression per cluster.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: var_names
params.desc: Should be a valid subset of `adata.var_names`. If `var_names` is a mapping, then the key is used as a label to group the values (see `var_group_labels`). The mapping values should be sequences of valid `adata.var_names`. In this case either coloring or ‘brackets’ are used for the grouping of var names depending on the plot. When `var_names` is a mapping, then the `var_group_labels` and `var_group_positions` are set.

params.name: groupby
params.desc: The key of the observation grouping to consider.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present.

params.name: log
params.desc: Plot on logarithmic axis.

params.name: num_categories
params.desc: Only used if groupby observation is not categorical. This value determines the number of groups into which the groupby observation should be subdivided.

params.name: categories_order
params.desc: Order in which to show the categories. Note: add_dendrogram or add_totals can change the categories order.

params.name: figsize
params.desc: Figure size when `multi_panel=True`. Otherwise the `rcParam['figure.figsize]` value is used. Format is (width, height).

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: var_group_positions
params.desc: Use this parameter to highlight groups of `var_names`. This will draw a ‘bracket’ or a color block between the given start and end positions. If the parameter `var_group_labels` is set, the corresponding labels are added on top/left.

params.name: var_group_labels
params.desc: Labels for each of the `var_group_positions` that want to be highlighted.

params.name: var_group_rotation
params.desc: Label rotation degrees. By default, labels larger than 4 characters are rotated 90 degrees.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Name of the AnnData object layer that wants to be plotted. By default `adata.raw.X` is plotted. If `use_raw=False` is set, then `adata.X` is plotted. If `layer` is set to a valid layer name, then the layer is plotted. `layer` takes precedence over `use_raw`.

params.name: standard_scale
params.desc: Whether or not to standardize a dimension between 0 and 1, meaning for each variable or observation, subtract the minimum and divide each by its maximum.

params.name: ax
params.desc: A matplotlib axes object. Only works if plotting a single component.

params.name: vmin
params.desc: Minimum value to use for colormap.

params.name: vmax
params.desc: Maximum value to use for colormap.

params.name: vcenter
params.desc: The value at which to center the colormap.

params.name: norm
params.desc: If not `None`, the normalization function to scale data before plotting. Otherwise, `vmin` and `vmax` are used.

params.name: kwds
params.desc: Additional keyword arguments are passed to `seaborn.violinplot()`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns an instance of the `StackedViolin` plot class.

misc:
### SEE ALSO
`stacked_violin()`
: simpler way to call `StackedViolin` but with less options.

`violin()`
: to plot marker genes identified using `rank_genes_groups()`

signature: scanpy.tools.dpt(adata, n_dcs=10, *, n_branchings=0, min_group_size=0.01, allow_kendall_tau_shift=True, neighbors_key=None, copy=False)
description: Infer progression of cells through geodesic distance along the graph. Reconstructs the progression of a biological process from snapshot data using Diffusion Pseudotime. It is designed to handle disconnected graphs and can run in a hierarchical mode by setting `n_branchings>1`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: n_dcs
params.desc: The number of diffusion components to use.

params.name: n_branchings
params.desc: Number of branchings to detect.

params.name: min_group_size
params.desc: During recursive splitting of branches for `n_branchings` > 1, do not consider groups that contain less than `min_group_size` data points. If a float, it refers to a fraction of the total number of data points.

params.name: allow_kendall_tau_shift
params.desc: If a very small branch is detected upon splitting, shift away from maximum correlation in Kendall tau criterion to stabilize the splitting.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: If not specified, dpt looks in .uns['neighbors'] for neighbor settings. If specified, it looks in the specified key for settings and connectivities.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Copy instance before computation and return a copy. Otherwise, perform computation inplace and return `None`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the fields: `adata.obs['dpt_pseudotime']`, an array storing the pseudotime of each cell, and `adata.obs['dpt_groups']`, an array storing subgroup IDs for each cell.

misc: The tool is similar to the R package `destiny` of Angerer et al. Added in version 1.1.

signature: scanpy.tools.ingest(adata, adata_ref, *, obs=None, embedding_method=('umap', 'pca'), labeling_method='knn', neighbors_key=None, inplace=True, **kwargs)
description: Map labels and embeddings from reference data to new data. Integrates embeddings and annotations of an `adata` with a reference dataset `adata_ref` through projecting on a PCA (or alternate model) that has been fitted on the reference data. The function uses a knn classifier for mapping labels and the UMAP package for mapping the embeddings.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes. This is the dataset without labels and embeddings.

params.name: adata_ref
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes. Variables (`n_vars` and `var_names`) of `adata_ref` should be the same as in `adata`. This is the dataset with labels and embeddings which need to be mapped to `adata`.

params.name: obs
params.type: str or list of str, optional
params.desc: Labels’ keys in `adata_ref.obs` which need to be mapped to `adata.obs` (inferred for observation of `adata`).

params.name: embedding_method
params.type: str or tuple of str, default=('umap', 'pca')
params.desc: Embeddings in `adata_ref` which need to be mapped to `adata`. The only supported values are ‘umap’ and ‘pca’.

params.name: labeling_method
params.desc: The method to map labels in `adata_ref.obs` to `adata.obs`. The only supported value is 'knn'.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: If not specified, ingest looks at `adata_ref.uns['neighbors']` for neighbors settings and `adata_ref.obsp['distances']` for distances. If specified, ingest looks at `adata_ref.uns[neighbors_key]` for neighbors settings and `adata_ref.obsp[adata_ref.uns[neighbors_key]['distances_key']]` for distances.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Only works if `return_joint=False`. Add labels and embeddings to the passed `adata` (if `True`) or return a copy of `adata` with mapped embeddings and labels.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `inplace=True`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.obs[obs]` with mapped labels, and `adata.obsm['X_umap' | 'X_pca']` with mapped embeddings ('X_umap' if `embedding_method` is 'umap', 'X_pca' if `embedding_method` is 'pca').

misc:
### Example

Call sequence:

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> sc.pp.neighbors(adata_ref)
>>> sc.tl.umap(adata_ref)
>>> sc.tl.ingest(adata, adata_ref, obs="cell_type")
```

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.log1p(data, *, base=None, copy=False, chunked=None, chunk_size=None, layer=None, obsm=None)
description:
Logarithmize the data matrix.

Computes $X = \log(X + 1)$, where $log$ denotes the natural logarithm unless a different base is given.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: base
params.desc: Base of the logarithm. Natural logarithm is used by default.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If an AnnData is passed, determines whether a copy is returned.

params.name: chunked
params.desc: Process the data matrix in chunks, which will save memory. Applies only to AnnData.

params.name: chunk_size
params.desc: `n_obs` of the chunks to process the data in.

params.name: layer
params.desc: Entry of layers to transform.

params.name: obsm

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.recipe_zheng17(adata, *, n_top_genes=1000, log=True, plot=False, copy=False)
description: Normalize and filter as of Zheng et al. [2017]. Reproduces the preprocessing of Zheng et al. [2017] – the Cell Ranger R Kit of 10x Genomics. Expects non-logarithmized data. If using logarithmized data, pass `log=False`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: n_top_genes
params.desc: Number of genes to keep.

params.name: log
params.desc: Take logarithm.

params.name: plot
params.desc: Show a plot of the gene dispersion vs. mean relation.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Return a copy of `adata` instead of updating it.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns or updates `adata` depending on `copy`.

misc:
The recipe runs the following steps

```
sc.pp.filter_genes(adata, min_counts=1)         # only consider genes with more than 1 count
sc.pp.normalize_per_cell(                       # normalize with total UMI count per cell
     adata, key_n_counts='n_counts_all'
)
filter_result = sc.pp.filter_genes_dispersion(  # select highly-variable genes
    adata.X, flavor='cell_ranger', n_top_genes=n_top_genes, log=False
)
adata = adata[:, filter_result.gene_subset]     # subset the genes
sc.pp.normalize_per_cell(adata)                 # renormalize after filtering
if log: sc.pp.log1p(adata)                      # log transform: adata.X = log(adata.X + 1)
sc.pp.scale(adata)                              # scale to unit variance and shift to zero mean
```

params.name: adata

params.name: color

params.name: gene_symbols
params.desc: Column name in `.var` DataFrame that stores gene symbols. By default `var_names` refer to the index column of the `.var` DataFrame. Setting this option allows alternative names to be used.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Use `.raw` attribute of `adata` for coloring with gene expression. If `None`, defaults to `True` if `layer` isn’t provided and `adata.raw` is present.

params.name: layer

params.name: edges
params.desc: Show edges.

params.name: edges_width
params.desc: Width of edges.

params.name: edges_color
params.desc: Color of edges. See `draw_networkx_edges()`.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: Where to look for neighbors connectivities. If not specified, this looks .obsp[‘connectivities’] for connectivities (default storage place for pp.neighbors). If specified, this looks .obsp[.uns[neighbors_key][‘connectivities_key’]] for connectivities.

params.name: arrows
params.desc: Show arrows (deprecated in favour of `scvelo.pl.velocity_embedding`).

params.name: arrows_kwds
params.desc: Passed to `quiver()`.

params.name: sort_order
params.desc: For continuous annotations used as color parameter, plot data points with higher values on top of others.

params.name: groups
params.desc: Restrict to a few categories in categorical observation annotation. The default is not to restrict to any groups.

params.name: dimensions
params.desc: 0-indexed dimensions of the embedding to plot as integers. E.g. [(0, 1), (1, 2)]. Unlike `components`, this argument is used in the same way as `colors`, e.g. is used to specify a single plot at a time. Will eventually replace the components argument.

params.name: components
params.desc: For instance, `['1,2', '2,3']`. To plot all available components use `components='all'`.

params.name: legend_loc
params.desc: Location of legend, either `'on data'`, `'right margin'`, `None`, or a valid keyword for the `loc` parameter of `Legend`.

params.name: legend_fontsize
params.desc: Numeric size in pt or string describing the size. See `set_fontsize()`.

params.name: legend_fontweight
params.desc: Legend font weight. A numeric value in range 0-1000 or a string. Defaults to `'bold'` if `legend_loc == 'on data'`, otherwise to `'normal'`. See `set_fontweight()`.

params.name: legend_fontoutline
params.desc: Line width of the legend font outline in pt. Draws a white outline using the path effect `withStroke`.

params.name: colorbar_loc
params.desc: Where to place the colorbar for continous variables. If `None`, no colorbar is added.

params.name: size
params.desc: Point size. If `None`, is automatically computed as 120000 / n_cells. Can be a sequence containing the size for each cell. The order should be the same as in adata.obs.

params.name: color_map
params.desc: Color map to use for continous variables. Can be a name or a `Colormap` instance (e.g. `'magma``, `'viridis'` or `mpl.cm.cividis`), see `get_cmap()`. If `None`, the value of `mpl.rcParams["image.cmap"]` is used. The default `color_map` can be set using `set_figure_params()`.

params.name: palette
params.desc: Colors to use for plotting categorical annotation groups. The palette can be a valid `ListedColormap` name (`'Set2'`, `'tab20'`, …), a `Cycler` object, a dict mapping categories to colors, or a sequence of colors. Colors must be valid to matplotlib. (see `is_color_like()`). If `None`, `mpl.rcParams["axes.prop_cycle"]` is used unless the categorical variable already has colors stored in `adata.uns["{var}_colors"]`. If provided, values of `adata.uns["{var}_colors"]` will be set.

params.name: na_color
params.desc: Color to use for null or masked values. Can be anything matplotlib accepts as a color. Used for all points if `color=None`.

params.name: na_in_legend
params.desc: If there are missing values, whether they get an entry in the legend. Currently only implemented for categorical legends.

params.name: frameon
params.desc: Draw a frame around the scatter plot. Defaults to value set in `set_figure_params()`, defaults to `True`.

params.name: title
params.desc: Provide title for panels either as string or list of strings, e.g. `['title1', 'title2', ...]`.

params.name: vmin
params.desc: The value representing the lower limit of the color scale. Values smaller than vmin are plotted with the same color as vmin. vmin can be a number, a string, a function or `None`. If vmin is a string and has the format `pN`, this is interpreted as a vmin=percentile(N). For example vmin=’p1.5’ is interpreted as the 1.5 percentile. If vmin is function, then vmin is interpreted as the return value of the function over the list of values to plot. For example to set vmin tp the mean of the values to plot, `def my_vmin(values): return np.mean(values)` and then set `vmin=my_vmin`. If vmin is None (default) an automatic minimum value is used as defined by matplotlib `scatter` function. When making multiple plots, vmin can be a list of values, one for each plot. For example `vmin=[0.1, 'p1', None, my_vmin]`

params.name: vmax
params.desc: The value representing the upper limit of the color scale. The format is the same as for `vmin`.

params.name: vcenter
params.desc: The value representing the center of the color scale. Useful for diverging colormaps. The format is the same as for `vmin`. Example: `sc.pl.umap(adata, color='TREM2', vcenter='p50', cmap='RdBu_r')`

params.name: add_outline
params.desc: If set to True, this will add a thin border around groups of dots. In some situations this can enhance the aesthetics of the resulting image

params.name: outline_color
params.desc: Tuple with two valid color names used to adjust the add_outline. The first color is the border color (default: black), while the second color is a gap color between the border color and the scatter dot (default: white).

params.name: outline_width
params.desc: Tuple with two width numbers used to adjust the outline. The first value is the width of the border color as a fraction of the scatter dot size (default: 0.3). The second value is width of the gap color (default: 0.05).

params.name: ncols
params.desc: Number of panels per row.

params.name: wspace
params.desc: Adjust the width of the space between multiple panels.

params.name: hspace
params.desc: Adjust the height of the space between multiple panels.

params.name: return_fig
params.desc: Return the matplotlib figure.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Arguments to pass to `matplotlib.pyplot.scatter()`, for instance: the maximum and minimum values (e.g. `vmin=-2, vmax=5`).

params.name: show
params.desc: Show the plot, do not return axis.

params.name: save
params.desc: If `True` or a `str`, save the figure. A string is appended to the default filename. Infer the filetype if ending on {`'.pdf'`, `'.png'`, `'.svg'`}.

params.name: ax

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.scale(data, *, zero_center=True, max_value=None, copy=False, layer=None, obsm=None, mask_obs=None)
description: Scale data to unit variance and zero mean.

params.name: data
params.desc: The annotated data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: zero_center
params.desc: If `False`, omit zero-centering variables, which allows to handle sparse input efficiently.

params.name: max_value
params.desc: Clip (truncate) to this value after scaling. If `None`, do not clip.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Whether this function should be performed inplace. If an AnnData object is passed, this also determines if a copy is returned.

params.name: layer
params.desc: If provided, which element of layers to scale.

params.name: obsm
params.desc: If provided, which element of obsm to scale.

params.name: mask_obs
params.desc: Restrict both the derivation of scaling parameters and the scaling itself to a certain set of observations. The mask is specified as a boolean array or a string referring to an array in `obs`. This will transform data from csc to csr format if `issparse(data)`.

params.name: return
params.type: AnnData | csr_matrix | csc_matrix | ndarray | Array | None
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an updated `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.X` | `adata.layers[layer]`: Scaled count data matrix. `adata.var['mean']`: Means per gene before scaling. `adata.var['std']`: Standard deviations per gene before scaling. `adata.var['var']`: Variances per gene before scaling.

misc: Variables (genes) that do not display any variation (are constant across all observations) are retained and (for zero_center==True) set to 0 during this operation. In the future, they might be set to NaNs.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.pca(data, n_comps=None, *, layer=None, zero_center=True, svd_solver=None, random_state=0, return_info=False, mask_var=_empty, use_highly_variable=None, dtype='float32', chunked=False, chunk_size=None, key_added=None, copy=False)
description: Principal component analysis (PCA) computes PCA coordinates, loadings, and variance decomposition using the implementation from scikit-learn.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape n_obs × n_vars. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: n_comps
params.desc: Number of principal components to compute. Defaults to 50, or 1 - minimum dimension size of selected representation.

params.name: layer
params.desc: If provided, specifies which element of layers to use for PCA.

params.name: zero_center
params.desc: If True, compute standard PCA from covariance matrix. If False, omit zero-centering variables.

params.name: svd_solver
params.desc: SVD solver to use. Options include 'arpack', 'covariance_eigh', 'randomized', 'auto', and 'tsqr'. Default depends on the class and the type of X.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Seed used by the random number generator.

params.name: return_info
params.desc: If True, returns additional information about fitted model.

params.name: mask_var
params.desc: Specifies a set of genes to use based on a boolean array or a string referring to an array in var.

params.name: use_highly_variable
params.desc: Whether to use highly variable genes only. Deprecated since version 1.10.0: Use mask_var instead.

params.name: dtype
params.desc: Numpy data type string to which to convert the result.

params.name: chunked
params.desc: If True, perform incremental PCA on segments of chunk_size.

params.name: chunk_size
params.desc: Number of observations to include in each chunk. Required if chunked=True.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: Key under which PCA results are stored in AnnData object.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If an AnnData is passed, determines whether a copy is returned.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns updated AnnData object if copy=True, else None. If data is array-like, returns PCA representation of data.

misc: Efficient computation of principal components of a sparse matrix currently only works with the 'arpack' or 'covariance_eigh' solver.

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.filter_cells(data, *, min_counts=None, min_genes=None, max_counts=None, max_genes=None, inplace=True, copy=False)
description: Filter cell outliers based on counts and numbers of genes expressed.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: min_counts
params.desc: Minimum number of counts required for a cell to pass filtering.

params.name: min_genes
params.desc: Minimum number of genes expressed required for a cell to pass filtering.

params.name: max_counts
params.desc: Maximum number of counts required for a cell to pass filtering.

params.name: max_genes
params.desc: Maximum number of genes expressed required for a cell to pass filtering.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: Perform computation inplace or return result.

params.name: return
params.desc:
Depending on `inplace`, returns the following arrays or directly subsets and annotates the data matrix:
- **cells_subset**: Boolean index mask that does filtering. `True` means that the cell is kept. `False` means the cell is removed.
- **number_per_cell**: Depending on what was thresholded (`counts` or `genes`), the array stores `n_counts` or `n_cells` per gene.

misc: For instance, only keep cells with at least `min_counts` counts or `min_genes` genes expressed. This is to filter measurement outliers, i.e. “unreliable” observations. Only provide one of the optional parameters `min_counts`, `min_genes`, `max_counts`, `max_genes` per call.

signature: scanpy.tools.leiden(adata, resolution=1, *, restrict_to=None, random_state=0, key_added='leiden', adjacency=None, directed=None, use_weights=True, n_iterations=-1, partition_type=None, neighbors_key=None, obsp=None, copy=False, flavor='leidenalg', **clustering_args)
description: Cluster cells into subgroups using the Leiden algorithm, an improved version of the Louvain algorithm. It was proposed for single-cell analysis by Levine et al. This requires having run `neighbors()` or `bbknn()` first.

params.name: adata
params.desc: The annotated data matrix.

params.name: resolution
params.desc: A parameter value controlling the coarseness of the clustering. Higher values lead to more clusters. Set to `None` if overriding `partition_type` to one that doesn’t accept a `resolution_parameter`.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: Change the initialization of the optimization.

params.name: restrict_to
params.desc: Restrict the clustering to the categories within the key for sample annotation, tuple needs to contain `(obs_key, list_of_categories)`.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: `adata.obs` key under which to add the cluster labels.

params.name: adjacency
params.desc: Sparse adjacency matrix of the graph, defaults to neighbors connectivities.

params.name: directed
params.desc: Whether to treat the graph as directed or undirected.

params.name: use_weights
params.desc: If `True`, edge weights from the graph are used in the computation (placing more emphasis on stronger edges).

params.name: n_iterations
params.desc: How many iterations of the Leiden clustering algorithm to perform. Positive values above 2 define the total number of iterations to perform, -1 has the algorithm run until it reaches its optimal clustering. 2 is faster and the default for underlying packages.

params.name: partition_type
params.desc: Type of partition to use. Defaults to `RBConfigurationVertexPartition`. For the available options, consult the documentation for `find_partition()`.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: Use neighbors connectivities as adjacency. If not specified, leiden looks at .obsp['connectivities'] for connectivities (default storage place for `pp.neighbors`). If specified, leiden looks at .obsp[.uns[neighbors_key]['connectivities_key']] for connectivities.

params.name: obsp
params.desc: Use .obsp[obsp] as adjacency. You can’t specify both `obsp` and `neighbors_key` at the same time.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Whether to copy `adata` or modify it inplace.

params.name: flavor
params.desc: Which package’s implementation to use.

params.name: **clustering_args
params.desc: Any further arguments to pass to `find_partition()` (which in turn passes arguments to the `partition_type`) or `igraph.Graph.community_leiden()` from `igraph`.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `copy=False`, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields: `adata.obs['leiden' | key_added]`: Array of dim (number of samples) that stores the subgroup id (`'0'`, `'1'`, …) for each cell. `adata.uns['leiden' | key_added]['params']`: A dict with the values for the parameters `resolution`, `random_state`, and `n_iterations`.

misc: Cluster cells using the Leiden algorithm. Enhanced version of the Louvain algorithm focusing on single-cell analysis. Implementations available with different parameter settings for customization and optimization. Use `neighbors()` or `bbknn()` before clustering.

signature: scanpy.tools.diffmap(adata, n_comps=15, *, neighbors_key=None, random_state=0, copy=False)
description: Diffusion Maps use adapted Gaussian kernel suggested by Haghverdi with the implementation of Wolf for visualizing single-cell data.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix.

params.name: n_comps
params.desc: The number of dimensions of the representation.

params.name: neighbors_key
params.desc: Key to look for neighbors settings and connectivities/distances in `adata.obs`.

params.name: random_state
params.desc: A numpy random seed.

params.name: copy
params.desc: Return a copy instead of writing to `adata`.

params.name: return
params.desc:
Returns `None` if copy=False, else returns an `AnnData` object. Sets the following fields:

`adata.obsm['X_diffmap']`: Diffusion map representation of data, which is the right eigen basis of the transition matrix with eigenvectors as columns.
`adata.uns['diffmap_evals']`: Array of size (number of eigen vectors). Eigenvalues of transition matrix.

misc: The 0-th column in `adata.obsm["X_diffmap"]` is the steady-state solution, which is non-informative in diffusion maps. Therefore, the first diffusion component is at index 1, e.g. `adata.obsm["X_diffmap"][:,1]`.

params.desc: Keys from either `.var_names`, `.var[gene_symbols]`, or `.obs.columns`.

params.desc: Tuples of `(key from obsm, column index of obsm[key])`.

misc:
### Examples

Getting value for plotting:

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> pbmc = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
>>> plotdf = sc.get.obs_df(
...     pbmc, keys=["CD8B", "n_genes"], obsm_keys=[("X_umap", 0), ("X_umap", 1)]
... )
>>> plotdf.columns
Index(['CD8B', 'n_genes', 'X_umap-0', 'X_umap-1'], dtype='object')
>>> plotdf.plot.scatter("X_umap-0", "X_umap-1", c="CD8B")  
<Axes: xlabel='X_umap-0', ylabel='X_umap-1'>
```

Calculating mean expression for marker genes by cluster:

```pycon
>>> pbmc = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
>>> marker_genes = ["CD79A", "MS4A1", "CD8A", "CD8B", "LYZ"]
>>> genedf = sc.get.obs_df(pbmc, keys=["louvain", *marker_genes])
>>> grouped = genedf.groupby("louvain", observed=True)
>>> mean, var = grouped.mean(), grouped.var()
```

signature: scanpy.tools.dendrogram(adata, groupby, *, n_pcs=None, use_rep=None, var_names=None, use_raw=None, cor_method='pearson', linkage_method='complete', optimal_ordering=False, key_added=None, inplace=True)
description:
Compute a hierarchical clustering for the given `groupby` categories.

By default, the PCA representation is used unless `.X` has less than 50 variables. Alternatively, a list of `var_names` (e.g. genes) can be given. Average values of either `var_names` or components are used to compute a correlation matrix.

The hierarchical clustering can be visualized using `scanpy.pl.dendrogram()` or multiple other visualizations that can include a dendrogram: `matrixplot()`, `heatmap()`, `dotplot()`, and `stacked_violin()`.

params.name: adata
params.desc: Annotated data matrix

params.name: n_pcs
params.desc: Use this many PCs. If `n_pcs==0` use `.X` if `use_rep is None`.

params.name: use_rep
params.desc: Use the indicated representation. `'X'` or any key for `.obsm` is valid. If `None`, the representation is chosen automatically: For `.n_vars` < `N_PCS` (default: 50), `.X` is used, otherwise ‘X_pca’ is used. If ‘X_pca’ is not present, it’s computed with default parameters or `n_pcs` if present.

params.name: var_names
params.desc: List of var_names to use for computing the hierarchical clustering. If `var_names` is given, then `use_rep` and `n_pcs` are ignored.

params.name: use_raw
params.desc: Only when `var_names` is not None. Use `raw` attribute of `adata` if present.

params.name: cor_method
params.desc: Correlation method to use. Options are ‘pearson’, ‘kendall’, and ‘spearman’

params.name: linkage_method
params.desc: Linkage method to use.

params.name: optimal_ordering
params.desc: Same as the optimal_ordering argument of `scipy.cluster.hierarchy.linkage()` which reorders the linkage matrix so that the distance between successive leaves is minimal.

params.name: key_added
params.desc: By default, the dendrogram information is added to `.uns[f'dendrogram_{groupby}']`. Notice that the `groupby` information is added to the dendrogram.

params.name: inplace
params.desc: If `True`, adds dendrogram information to `adata.uns[key_added]`, else this function returns the information.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns `None` if `inplace=True`, else returns a `dict` with dendrogram information. Sets the following field if `inplace=True`: `adata.uns[f'dendrogram_{group_by}' | key_added]`: Dendrogram information.

misc:
### Examples

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> adata = sc.datasets.pbmc68k_reduced()
>>> sc.tl.dendrogram(adata, groupby="bulk_labels")
>>> sc.pl.dendrogram(adata, groupby="bulk_labels")
<Axes: >
>>> markers = ["C1QA", "PSAP", "CD79A", "CD79B", "CST3", "LYZ"]
>>> sc.pl.dotplot(adata, markers, groupby="bulk_labels", dendrogram=True)
```

signature: scanpy.preprocessing.sqrt(data, *, copy=False, chunked=False, chunk_size=None)
description: Take square root of the data matrix. Computes $X = \sqrt(X)$.

params.name: data
params.desc: The (annotated) data matrix of shape `n_obs` × `n_vars`. Rows correspond to cells and columns to genes.

params.name: copy
params.desc: If an `AnnData` object is passed, determines whether a copy is returned.

params.name: chunked
params.desc: Process the data matrix in chunks, which will save memory. Applies only to `AnnData`.

params.name: chunk_size
params.desc: `n_obs` of the chunks to process the data in.

params.name: return
params.desc: Returns or updates `data`, depending on `copy`.

signature: scanpy.queries.biomart_annotations(org, attrs, *, host='www.ensembl.org', use_cache=False)
description: Retrieve gene annotations from ensembl biomart.

params.name: org
params.desc: Organism to query. Must be an organism in ensembl biomart. Examples include 'hsapiens', 'mmusculus', 'drerio', etc.

params.name: attrs
params.desc: Attributes to query biomart for.

params.name: host
params.desc: A valid BioMart host URL. Alternative values include archive urls (like 'grch37.ensembl.org') or regional mirrors (like 'useast.ensembl.org').

params.name: use_cache
params.desc: Whether pybiomart should use a cache for requests. Will create a `.pybiomart.sqlite` file in current directory if used.

params.name: return
params.desc: Dataframe containing annotations.

misc:
### Examples

Retrieve genes coordinates and chromosomes

```pycon
>>> import scanpy as sc
>>> annot = sc.queries.biomart_annotations(
...     "hsapiens",
...     ["ensembl_gene_id", "start_position", "end_position", "chromosome_name"],
... ).set_index("ensembl_gene_id")
>>> adata.var[annot.columns] = annot
```
